name: Big Flat Parking Area
note: Designated drive-up/roadside campsites in Michaux State Forest (Big Flat area, off Birch Run Rd). Reservation + permit genuinely REQUIRED -- book via the PA State Parks & Forests reservation system (ReserveAmerica) or 888-PA-PARKS (888-727-2757); designated sites only. One wheeled camping unit (RV/trailer/pop-up) per site -- RVs accommodated, some pull-through (verify length for a 23-ft rig). No hookups, dump, or trash service (pack out). Campfires prohibited Mar 1-May 25. Forest-road access; scout ahead. Michaux district office 717-352-2211. No water body adjacent. --Claude

note: Electric riverfront sites front the Maumee River (e.g. pull-through site 8); 4 premium sites have electric + water.  RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mosquito Lake State Park
note: Electric lakeshore sites face Mosquito Creek Lake (e.g. site 67, cul-de-sac 214-224, 222).  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mount Gilead State Park
note: RV Life 1*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Muskingum River Parkway State Park (Ellis Lock 11)
note: 20 non-electric riverside sites at Ellis Lock #11; RVs/trailers up to 30 feet. Open April-October. (The park's Luke Chute Lock #5 sites are boat-in only.)  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Paint Creek State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Pike Lake State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Punderson State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Rocky Fork State Park
note: Lower Lakeside camping area: premium full-hookup sites 301-320 and electric sites 203-216 sit directly on Rocky Fork Lake.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Salt Fork State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Scioto Trail State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Shawnee State Park
note: RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Stonelick State Park
note: Premium electric sites 24-30 (plus 53, 55) sit on the Stonelick Lake shoreline ('Shoreline Access' loop).  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Strouds Run State Park
note: RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tar Hollow State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Van Buren State Park
note: RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wolf Run State Park
note: Electric peninsula sites in Camping Area 2 front Wolf Run Lake on both sides; premium lakeside sites 22-26 are non-electric.  RV Life 3*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mill Creek Recreation Area (Berlin Lake)
note: USACE-operated (Pittsburgh District) on Berlin Lake. 295 sites, 100+ electric; some are wooded lakefront water sites. Booked online.  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Leith Run Recreation Area (Wayne National Forest)
note: Wayne National Forest (USFS), Marietta Unit. On the north bank of the Ohio River, 18 mi N of Marietta on SR-7; official description lists many waterfront sites. Electric sites, flush toilets, showers, dump station. Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Iron Ridge Campground (Lake Vesuvius, Wayne National Forest)
note: Wayne National Forest (USFS), Lake Vesuvius Recreation Area (Ironton RD). 32 sites (~21 electric/RV); on a wooded ridge above the lake with a 1/2-mi trail to the water (not lakefront). Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Oak Hill Campground (Lake Vesuvius, Wayne National Forest)
note: Wayne National Forest (USFS), Lake Vesuvius Recreation Area (Ironton RD). Renovated; water + electric hookups and flush toilets. On a ridge on the lake's west side, within walking distance of Lake Vesuvius (beach ~10-min drive) - not lakefront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hidden Hollow Campground (Fernwood State Forest)
note: ODNR Division of Forestry. 22 primitive family sites (paved), tents or RVs, no hookups; front-of-loop sites suit small/medium rigs (sites are small and close together). First-come, first-served, free; self-register.  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ronsheim Campground (Harrison State Forest)
note: ODNR Division of Forestry. 7 family sites with paved trailer pads; paved access road handles small/medium RVs. No hookups; vault latrines, no potable water. First-come, first-served, free; self-register. (Separate Trailriders horse camp excluded.)  RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: East Fork Campground (East Lynn Lake)
note: USACE (Huntington District) on East Lynn Lake. 167 sites, most with electric + water hookups; flush toilets, showers, dump station, boat ramp. Has a waterfront-campsite fee tier (premium sites directly on the lake). Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Guyandotte Campground (R.D. Bailey Lake)
note: USACE (Huntington District) on R.D. Bailey Lake. 94 sites, electric to full hookup; showers, dump station. First-come, first-served (no reservations); open May-Oct.

name: Riffle Run Campground (Burnsville Lake)
note: USACE (Huntington District) on Burnsville Lake. 60 sites (54 full-hookup 50-amp + 6 primitive); showers, laundry. First-come, first-served (no reservations); open late Apr-Thanksgiving.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Bee Run Campground (Sutton Lake)
note: USACE (Huntington District) on Sutton Lake. 12 primitive hilltop sites suitable for RVs/trailers/tents; vault toilets, no hookups or potable water. First-come, first-served (no reservations).

name: Pocahontas Campground (Monongahela National Forest)
note: USFS, Monongahela NF (Marlinton-White Sulphur RD). 8 small primitive sites for tents or campers, paved SR-92 access; vault toilets, no potable water or hookups. First-come, first-served, self-pay.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: South Shore Recreation Area
note: USACE campground on the south shore of Cowanesque Lake (Baltimore District). Reservable online; electric sites. Lakeside setting but sites sit above the water, not lakefront (Tompkins is the lakefront campground here). Access road can be rough for larger rigs.

name: Outflow Campground (Youghiogheny River Lake)
note: USACE campground at the base of the Youghiogheny River Lake dam (Pittsburgh District), along the river outflow and the Great Allegheny Passage rail trail. Reservable online; electric RV sites near (not on) the water.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tionesta Recreation Area
note: USACE campground below Tionesta Dam along Tionesta Creek (Pittsburgh District). Reservable online; standard electric sites, ~40 ft max. A few sites (e.g. 46, 48) back up above the creek; not step-to-water lakefront.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tionesta Outflow Recreation Area
note: USACE campground on the outflow side of Tionesta Dam along Tionesta Creek (Pittsburgh District). Reservable online; full-hookup and standard sites. Some sites back toward the creek. Reviewer-reported dirt speed bumps can jar RVs.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: East Branch Campground
note: USACE campground on a wooded hillside near East Branch Clarion River Lake (Pittsburgh District). Reservable online; 16 electric + 16 non-electric sites plus cabins. Steep terrain, ~30 ft RV max, 13'4" powerline clearance at the entrance. Not lakefront.  RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Milton Loop Campground
note: County campground on Mahoning Creek Lake (USACE Pittsburgh District flood-control project), leased to Armstrong County and run by a concessionaire. Electric + primitive sites and cabins, big-rig friendly; dump station, no sewer hookups. Reserve by phone — no online booking. Separated from the water by a band of woods — no real water view and no direct water access.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Curwensville Lake Campground
note: Clearfield County recreation area on Curwensville Lake (USACE Baltimore District project). Reservable online. 46-site electric RV loop (electric only, no water/sewer; dump station). About a dozen no-hookup waterfront sites sit right on the lake; the electric RV sites are set back from the water.

name: Dewdrop Campground
note: Allegheny NF campground on the Kinzua Bay shoreline of the Allegheny Reservoir. Reservable online; flush toilets, dump station, no hookups. Several sites sit on/near the bay with direct water access (kayak launch steps from camp).  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tracy Ridge Campground
note: Allegheny NF ridge-top campground, a base for the Tracy Ridge trail system above the Allegheny Reservoir. Reservable online in summer / FCFS in shoulder season; dump station, no hookups; sites fit RVs ~25-60 ft. Not on the water.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Twin Lakes Campground
note: Allegheny NF campground at spring-fed Twin Lake (not the reservoir), with a swimming beach and fishing piers. Reservations via Friends of Twin Lakes. Caution: ~10-ft height limit at a railroad underpass on the entrance road -- verify RV routing. Near the lake, not lakefront.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Loleta Recreation Area
note: Allegheny NF campground along East Branch Millstone Creek with a pond and small beach. Reservable online; Lower Loop has 30-amp electric and fits larger RVs (Upper Loop not recommended for big rigs). Creekside, not on a lake.  RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Buckaloons Recreation Area
note: Allegheny NF campground at the confluence of the Allegheny River and Brokenstraw Creek near Irvine. Reservable online; electric hookups, hot showers, dump station. Riverside access; sites not confirmed directly on the water.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hearts Content Recreation Area
note: Allegheny NF campground beside the Hearts Content old-growth area and Hickory Creek Wilderness. Reservable online; 26 sites, dump station, no hookups. Smaller sites -- verify length for an RV. Not on the water.  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Minister Creek Campground
note: Small Allegheny NF campground along Minister Creek on PA-666, a popular trailhead. First-come, first-served; 6 sites, no hookups, tents and small RVs only. Creekside.  RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Kellettville Recreation Area
note: USACE Tionesta Lake campground along Tionesta Creek within the Allegheny NF (Pittsburgh District). Reservable online; ~20 sites, 19 fitting RVs ~35-60 ft. Some sites along the creek.

name: Dingmans Campground
note: Privately operated under NPS authorization, on federal land within Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area. Book direct (not Recreation.gov). Riverside sites sit directly on the Delaware River; forest RV sites (20/30/50-amp electric + water) are set back. ~40-ft RV max.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Little Buffalo State Park
note: DCNR state park campground near 88-acre Holman Lake. Reservable via the PA reservation system; ~40 sites including water + 50-amp electric sites, 2 full-hookup back-in RV sites, and a dump station (plus camping cottages/cabin). Sites sit south of the lake, not lakefront.  RV Life 3.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Colton Point State Park
note: DCNR state park on the WEST rim of the Pine Creek Gorge (PA Grand Canyon), across from Leonard Harrison. First-come, first-served; 25 rustic sites, ~7 fit RVs up to 38 ft, no hookups or dump station, non-flush toilets. Gorge rim views.  RV Life 3*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Denton Hill State Park (Nine Mile Loop)
note: DCNR state park campground (Nine Mile Loop) along Nine Mile Creek, operated by the Denton Go resort under DCNR. 11 gravel sites with electric, flush toilets and hot showers; reservable via Denton Go. One of PA's highest parks; verify max RV length.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Asaph Campground
note: Developed drive-in state forest campground (Tioga State Forest) along Asaph Run near Gaines. 16 sites with picnic tables, fire rings, and latrines; no hookups. Reservable via the PA reservation system (required as of 2024).

name: County Bridge Campground
note: Developed drive-in state forest campground (Tioga State Forest) near Grover, along a small stream. ~14 sites, no hookups; some sites fit small RVs (verify 23-ft fit). Reservable via the PA reservation system (required as of 2024).

name: Brown County State Park
note: Buffalo Ridge campground: ~100+ electric (30/50-amp) drive-in sites; Ogle Lake is nearby but the campground sits on a ridge above it, not lakefront. RVs should use the west (SR 46) entrance — height/weight limit at the north gatehouse. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Chain O'Lakes State Park
note: ~331 electric + 49 non-electric + 33 primitive drive-in sites. The chain of lakes is in the park but the modern campground is not on a lake (the only on-water sites are canoe-in primitive). Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Charlestown State Park
note: ~60 full-hookup + ~132 electric sites, paved pads. Campground sits on the upland plateau; the Ohio River and Fourteenmile Creek are well below via bluffs, not at the sites. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Clifty Falls State Park
note: ~99 electric (50-amp, many pull-through) + ~62 non-electric sites. Campground is upland, above the gorge/Ohio River valley (creek and falls are trail-access only), not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Harmonie State Park
note: 200-site electric family campground (50-amp accessible sites, pull-throughs). On the Wabash River (boat ramp) but the campground is set back in the hills, not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lincoln State Park
note: Lake Lincoln campground: 150 electric + 121 primitive drive-in sites. Lakeside sites (e.g. 40, 41) are view-only with a trail/buffer to the water, not at the water's edge. (Lake was drawn down for 2025 dam repairs and refilling.) Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mounds State Park
note: ~75 electric drive-in sites (pads to ~40+ ft), bathhouse + dump station. The White River runs through the park but the campground is on the bluff/upland above it, not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: O'Bannon Woods State Park
note: ~281 electric drive-in sites on a hardwood ridge top, well above the Ohio and Blue rivers — not waterfront. (Separate equestrian and primitive camps excluded.) Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ouabache State Park
note: ~124 electric drive-in sites, modern restrooms, dump station. 25-acre Kunkel Lake is a separate feature with its own beach; the campground is inland, not lakefront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Potato Creek State Park
note: 287 electric drive-in sites (pads to ~60 ft). 327-acre Worster Lake is the park centerpiece but the campground is set back inland from the shoreline (woods/trails between), not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Prophetstown State Park
note: ~55 full-hookup + ~55 electric (50-amp) drive-in sites on restored tallgrass prairie, set well back from the Wabash/Tippecanoe confluence — not waterfront. Reservable online (not first-come).  RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Shades State Park
note: ~105 non-electric drive-in sites (no hookups) with dump station, water, and showers; accommodates RVs/trailers. Sits on the clifftop high above Sugar Creek, not waterfront. Reservable online. Open April-October.  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Spring Mill State Park
note: 175 electric (Class A, 30-amp) + 44 full-hookup sites, 20 pull-throughs. Spring Mill Lake (no swimming) is west of the campground; sites are not lakefront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Summit Lake State Park
note: 120 electric (50-amp) sites, many with water. Several lakefront sites (e.g. 38, 40, 97, 98) sit directly on ~800-acre Summit Lake — dock/launch right from the site. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tippecanoe River State Park
note: Family campground: 112 electric (20/30/50-amp) drive-in sites, set back from the river. The only on-river sites are the tent-only River Tent Camp (no RVs), so the RV campground is not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Turkey Run State Park
note: 213 electric (Class A) drive-in sites in two paved loops navigable by big rigs. Sugar Creek flows through the park but the campground is on upland west of the creek, not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Versailles State Park
note: ~226 electric (30-amp) drive-in sites in loops A/B/C, paved pads. 230-acre Versailles Lake is a short walk away but the family campgrounds are set back (only the group camp is on the bank), not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Whitewater Memorial State Park
note: Family campground: 236 electric (20/30-amp) + 45 non-electric drive-in sites, 12 pull-throughs. 200-acre Whitewater Lake is reached via ~1-mi trail; campground is not lakefront. (Separate horsemen's camp excluded.) Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mounds State Recreation Area (Brookville Lake)
note: Indiana DNR, Mounds SRA on Brookville Lake. ~345 drive-in sites, electric (20/30/50A) plus some full-hookup big-rig sites. Campground set back from the lake (shared beach access), not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Quakertown State Recreation Area (Brookville Lake)
note: Indiana DNR, Quakertown SRA at the north end of Brookville Lake. 91 electric (20/30/50A) drive-in sites, RVs to 60 ft. Not waterfront. Reservable online. (Dunlapsville Rd bridge closed for an INDOT project through the 2026 season.)  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lieber State Recreation Area (Cagles Mill Lake)
note: Indiana DNR, Lieber SRA (Poplar Grove campground) on Cagles Mill Lake. ~115 Class A electric + ~94 Class B non-electric drive-in sites. Campground sits back in the treeline, not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Raccoon State Recreation Area (Cecil M. Harden Lake)
note: Indiana DNR, Raccoon SRA on Cecil M. Harden Lake (Mansfield Reservoir). ~150+ drive-in sites: non-electric / 30A / 50A full-hookup; many need leveling. The 'Lakeview' loop faces the lake (partly tree-screened views, steep access) but no sites are at the water's edge. Reservable online.  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hardy Lake State Recreation Area
note: Indiana DNR, Hardy Lake SRA (Shale Bluff campground). 149 electric (30/50A) drive-in sites on a bluff above the lake — filtered lake views with trails down to the water, not at the water's edge. Reservable online. (The genuinely on-water sites are in the separate Wooster primitive campground.)  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lost Bridge West State Recreation Area (Salamonie Lake)
note: Indiana DNR, Lost Bridge West SRA (Modern campground) on Salamonie Lake. ~243 electric (30/50A) drive-in sites; some sit on the water (best lake access at sites 228, 232). Reservable online. (Lost Bridge East and Apple Orchard primitive areas excluded.)  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Deam Lake State Recreation Area
note: Indiana DNR, Deam Lake SRA family campground (within Clark State Forest). 113 electric (50A) drive-in sites, modern restrooms/showers. 194-acre lake with a beach, but the family campground is not waterfront. Reservable online. (Separate equestrian loops excluded.)  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Starve Hollow State Recreation Area
note: Indiana DNR, Starve Hollow SRA (within Jackson-Washington State Forest), Vallonia. 90 electric + 55 full-hookup drive-in sites; some sit right on the lakeshore. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Clark State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Clark State Forest family campground. 40 non-electric drive-in sites, dump station. Seven small lakes on the property but sites are not on a shore. First-come, first-served (self-register). (Separate equestrian campground excluded.)  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ferdinand State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Ferdinand State Forest family campground (~57 sites). Loops A & B sit on the north shore of Ferdinand Lake (beach/boat launch); loops C-E are set back. Drive-in, first-come, first-served (self-register). Note: a 2025-26 renovation is adding electric and paved pads.  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Greene-Sullivan State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Greene-Sullivan State Forest (Narrow Lake area). ~100 family drive-in sites across reclaimed strip-mine lakes; 5 electric RV sites (P1-P3 sit on/near Narrow Lake). First-come, first-served (self-register). (Separate horseman's camp at Ladder Lake excluded.)  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Jackson-Washington State Forest (Knob Lake Campground)
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Jackson-Washington State Forest (Knob Lake campground). ~48 drive-in sites; some are waterfront on Knob Lake. First-come, first-served (self-register). (Youth tent area and Knobstone backcountry excluded.)  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Martin State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Martin State Forest family campground. 26 non-electric drive-in sites, self check-in. Three small lakes on the property but sites are not on a shore. First-come, first-served (no reservations).  RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Morgan-Monroe State Forest (Mason Ridge Campground)
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Morgan-Monroe State Forest (Mason Ridge campground, with Oak Ridge overflow). ~19 drive-in sites; the forest lakes are day-use only, campground not waterfront. First-come, first-served (register at the forest office).  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Owen-Putnam State Forest (Fish Creek Campground)
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Owen-Putnam State Forest (Fish Creek campground). 14 primitive drive-in sites, self-register. Ponds only, not waterfront. First-come, first-served. (Separate horse/equestrian camps excluded.)  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Pike State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Pike State Forest family campground. 11 primitive drive-in sites, self-register. First-come, first-served. (Separate horseman's campground excluded.)  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Salamonie River State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Salamonie River State Forest family campground. 21 primitive drive-in sites, self-register; near 4-acre Hominy Ridge Lake and the Salamonie River but not on a shore. First-come, first-served. (Separate horseman's camp excluded.)

name: Yellowwood State Forest Campground
note: Indiana DNR Division of Forestry, Yellowwood State Forest. ~79 primitive drive-in sites (gravel pads) in four loops adjacent to 133-acre Yellowwood Lake, with short paths down to the water; sites sit back above the shore, not at the water's edge. One dump station. First-come, first-served (register at the forest office). (Carry-in tent area and horseman's camp excluded.)  RV Life 3*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hardin Ridge Recreation Area (Hoosier National Forest)
note: USFS, Hoosier NF on Monroe Lake. ~200+ drive-in sites in six loops, mix of electric and non-electric, showerhouses; the largest Hoosier NF campground. On the reservoir but standard sites are set back in the loops, not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tipsaw Lake Recreation Area (Hoosier National Forest)
note: USFS, Hoosier NF on 131-acre Tipsaw Lake (electric motors only). ~60 drive-in sites in three loops (some electric/water), beach and boat ramp. Sites sit in the forest next to the lake, not at the water's edge. Reservable online.  RV Life 2.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Indian-Celina Lakes Recreation Area (Hoosier National Forest)
note: USFS, Hoosier NF on Celina and Indian Lakes. 63 drive-in sites in two loops (South Slope electric/seasonal; North Face mostly non-electric, year-round), flush toilets and showers. Sites set back in the woods, not waterfront. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Buzzard Roost Recreation Area (Hoosier National Forest)
note: USFS, Hoosier NF. 5 small primitive drive-in sites on a clifftop overlooking the Ohio River (river view, not on the water); tables/grills, vault toilet, no potable water. First-come, first-served, free. Sites are short — verify length for larger rigs.

name: Breaks Interstate Park Campground
note: Breaks Interstate Park, run by the VA-KY Breaks Interstate Park Commission (developed area is on the Virginia side). 138 drive-in sites in loops A-D: primitive, electric+water, and full-hookup; paved loops. Reservable (Firefly); full campground Apr 1-Oct 31, Loop D open off-season. Loops sit on the wooded plateau, not on the water (the Laurel Lake lakefront accommodations are separate cabins).  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Pound River Campground (Flannagan Reservoir)
note: USACE (Norfolk District) on John W. Flannagan Reservoir (Pound River arm), near Clintwood. 27 drive-in sites: 22 RV-electric + 5 tent (non-electric). Reservable online. Sites sit along the river arm but are not confirmed at the water's edge.

name: Lower Twin Campground (Flannagan Reservoir)
note: USACE (Norfolk District) on John W. Flannagan Reservoir, near the dam (Haysi). ~33 drive-in sites (RV-electric and tent-electric). Reservable online.  RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cranesnest Campground (Flannagan Reservoir)
note: USACE (Norfolk District) on John W. Flannagan Reservoir (Cranesnest River arm), near Clintwood. ~30 drive-in sites (RV-electric, tent-electric, and non-electric RV). Reservable online. NOTE: temporarily closed for construction (roadway/site resurfacing) - verify reopening before relying on it.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Jamison Mill Park (Philpott Lake)
note: USACE (Wilmington District) on Philpott Lake, near Henry. Small drive-in campground (~19 sites, no hookups per most sources; one source says 5 with electric/water - unverified), boat launch, wooded lakeside setting. First-come, first-served (not on recreation.gov). Season ~Apr-Oct.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lewis Mountain Campground
note: NPS - Shenandoah National Park, Skyline Drive milepost 57.5. Smallest of the park's four campgrounds (~30 wooded sites), no hookups, flush toilets, showers and a camp store on site. First-come, first-served (self-registration). High elevation.  RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wilderness Road Campground
note: NPS - Cumberland Gap National Historical Park, off US-58 near Ewing (Lee County, VA side of the park). ~151 sites with 43 electric (20/30/50-amp) hookups in loops B and C, hot showers and potable water. Reservable online.  RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Morris Hill Campground
note: USFS (Warm Springs/James River district) on a wooded ridge above the south end of Lake Moomaw near Covington; ~0.5 mi from the lakeshore (not on the water - swim beach, fishing pier and boat ramp are a short drive away). Drive-in family campground, reservable online.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Steel Bridge Campground
note: USFS (Eastern Divide district) near Paint Bank, in a narrow valley along Potts Creek (a stocked trout stream with a swimming hole). Three unpaved, shaded sites along a single dirt road that parallels the creek; the strip directly adjacent to the creek is designated day-use only, so the overnight sites are set back from the water rather than on it. No fee, first-come, first-served, open year-round. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Cane Patch Campground
note: USFS (Clinch district) near North Fork of Pound Lake, near Pound. ~35 sites including 7 with electric, RVs to ~32 ft. NOTE: currently closed - verify reopening before relying on it.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cave Springs Recreation Area
note: USFS (Clinch district) near Dryden; drive-in campground at a recreation area with a spring-fed swimming pond (campsites are not directly on the water). NOTE: currently closed - verify reopening before relying on it.  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Chesapeake Campground
note: 9.5/10★/9 (Good Sam). RV Life 4★, price rating $$ (2/4) - high-rated and affordable. Private campground near Chesapeake, VA. Sits in the Deep Creek area along the Dismal Swamp Canal with a boat ramp and canoe/kayak rentals, but the RV sites are inland/wooded with canal access only via the boat ramp (not site-frontage), so waterfront none. Distinct from the city-run Northwest River Park. Added by cross-referencing Good Sam-rated VA parks against RV Life (kept only >=4 stars, <=$$ price, not membership parks). --Claude

name: Cool Breeze Campground
note: 7.5/9.5★/9 (Good Sam). RV Life 4.5★, price rating $$ (2/4) - high-rated and affordable. Private campground near Galax, VA. Not waterfront - sits just off Blue Ridge Parkway MP 215; nearest water (New River / New River Trail) is ~6 mi away. Added by cross-referencing Good Sam-rated VA parks against RV Life (kept only >=4 stars, <=$$ price, not membership parks). --Claude

name: Meadows Of Dan Campground & Cabins
note: 6.5/9.5★/9 (Good Sam). RV Life 4.5★, price rating $$ (2/4) - high-rated and affordable. Private campground near Meadows of Dan, VA. Not waterfront - on the Blue Ridge crest near Mabry Mill (~3,000 ft); Dan River trout fishing is off-site. Added by cross-referencing Good Sam-rated VA parks against RV Life (kept only >=4 stars, <=$$ price, not membership parks). --Claude

name: Parkview RV Park
note: 5.5/0/7 (Good Sam). RV Life 4★, price rating $$ (2/4) - high-rated and affordable. Private RV park near Appomattox, VA. Not waterfront - level paved lot adjoining the Appomattox Court House battlefield. Added by cross-referencing Good Sam-rated VA parks against RV Life (kept only >=4 stars, <=$$ price, not membership parks). --Claude

name: Deep Branch Family Campground
note: RV Life 4★, price rating $$ (2/4). Private family campground near Milton, DE; open to the public, water/electric sites, RVs to ~30 ft, cabins and tent sites. Deep Branch (a shallow stream from Flaxhole Pond) runs along the property line, but no confirmation sites sit on the water, so waterfront none. Found via RV Life-first search (>=4 stars, <=$$, not membership). --Claude

name: Greenbelt Park Campground
note: NPS (National Capital Parks-East), in Greenbelt MD just outside Washington DC. ~172 wooded sites in 4 loops with paved spurs; RVs to ~30 ft (B loop) / ~35 ft (D loop), no hookups, flush toilets, showers and a dump station. Reservable year-round online. Not waterfront. RV Life 4.5★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Owens Creek Campground
note: NPS, Catoctin Mountain Park near Thurmont/Sabillasville MD. 48 wooded single sites, reservation-only (no walk-ins), no hookups. Mostly tent-oriented - only 3 pull-through sites (12/39/43) take RVs up to ~30 ft (trailers <=22 ft), so RV-usable but limited. Owens Creek (a small stream) runs through the area but sites are not on the water. --Claude  RV Life 3.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Bedford Visitor's Center Campground
note: Town of Bedford (local tourism/welcome center) overnight RV stop at the Bedford Area Welcome Center. Just 3 full-hookup (50-amp) sites, no tent sites. First-come, first-served, 1-night max, register before ~5pm. Roadside, not waterfront. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Cavitts Creek Park
note: Tazewell County park near 54-acre Lake Witten (Jack Witten). ~20 sites: full-hookup pull-thru RV, primitive RV/tent, plus cabins. Reservable, seasonal (~May-Sept). RV sites are in a gravel lot set back uphill from the lake with no reliable lake view (lake/trail a short walk away). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Cleveland RiverBend Campground
note: Town of Cleveland municipal campground on the Clinch River (opened 2021). RV sites (electric+water) + primitive tent sites, dump station, kayak/canoe slips, boardwalk to town park. Reservations required, 2-week max. On the river; site-level on-water placement not confirmed. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Explore Park
note: Roanoke County park at Blue Ridge Parkway MP 115; camping operated by a private concessionaire (Don's Cab-Inns) with reservations via Roanoke County. RV sites with 50-amp, 35-ft pads (no water/sewer at site; dump station), plus cabins, yurts, tent sites. Reservations, 2-night weekend min. RV area is up by the visitor center, not on the Roanoke River. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Glen Lyn Town Park
note: Town of Glen Lyn municipal park on the New River. Full-hookup (30/50-amp) RV sites on gravel/grass plus primitive and electric tent sites, bathhouse, boat ramp. First-come, first-served (register at Town Office). Open Mar 1-Nov 30. Many sites have river views. RV Life unrated, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Glen Maury Park
note: City of Buena Vista park (Glen Maury Park Authority) on the Maury River. ~142 sites - lower riverside loop (30-amp+water, big-rig friendly) and upper full-hookup loop, plus many tent sites along the river. First-come, first-served (no reservations). Tent sites sit directly along the river; lower RV loop overlooks it. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Lake A. Willis Robertson Campground
note: Rockbridge County recreation area near 26-acre Lake Robertson (a DWR fishing lake). ~53 sites, all electric+water, 7 pull-thrus, RVs to ~33-40 ft. Reservable, open Apr-Oct. The campground sits on hillside loops set apart from the lake (reached by a separate road); RV sites do not front or overlook the water. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Leeman Field Campground
note: Town of Pennington Gap municipal RV park at the Leeman Field fairgrounds, near the North Fork of the Powell River. 21 full-hookup (30/50-amp) sites, RVs to ~45 ft, plus tent camping along the river. Reservations (Campspot). The RV pads sit in the open fairground lot ~150-250 m from the river with no river view; only the tent area is near the water. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Natural Chimneys Park and Campground
note: Augusta County park on the North River. 165 sites (96 30-amp, 12 50-amp, 36 both, water+electric; dump station) plus a riverside tent section. Reservations encouraged, walk-ups welcome. Only the primitive tent (T) sites run along the river (behind the River's Edge Trail); the electric RV loops are set back in woods/field with no river view (the North River can run dry mid-summer). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Northwest River Park Campground
note: City of Chesapeake park (Parks, Rec & Tourism) on the 763-acre Northwest River park. ~72 family sites (~44 with 30/50-amp electric, shared water, dump station) + group sites and 2 cabins; RVs to ~50 ft. Reservations (Firefly). Wooded inland sites; river access via trail (sites not on the water). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Rural Retreat Lake Campground
note: Wythe County park near 90-acre Rural Retreat Lake. ~72 sites - primitive/tent and 30/50-amp water+electric RV sites (rigs to ~42 ft), disc golf, seasonal pool. Mixed reservations (Campspot) and first-come, first-served. Campground phone is seasonal: 276-686-4331 (May-Sept) / 276-223-4517 (Oct-Apr). RV/hookup sites are wooded and set back from the lake (reached by trail/boat launch); they do not front or overlook the water. RV Life 3.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Sugar Hollow Park Campground
note: City of Bristol park (~400 acres) along Beaver Creek. 75 electric (30/50-amp) sites, no tent camping (except Rhythm & Roots weekend), no cabins. First-come, first-served, pay at entrance booth. Creekside trail; the mapped 'Beaver Creek Lake' is a TVA dry flood basin, not a standing lake. RV Life 4★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Washington County Park
note: Washington County Park Authority park on South Holston Lake (lake is TVA, park is county-run). ~140 full-hookup (30/50-amp) RV sites of all sizes (pull-thrus, to ~45 ft) plus tent sites, 7 lakefront shelter sites, swimming beach, 2 boat launches. Reservations via the Park Authority. The campground fills a narrow lake peninsula - outer-loop full-hookup RV sites back directly onto the lake (some with docks); inner sites have lake views. RV Life 5★, price $$$ (3/4). --Claude

name: Little Bennett Campground
note: Montgomery County / M-NCPPC (Little Bennett Regional Park), Clarksburg MD - the county's only public campground. 91 wooded sites incl. 22 full-hookup (water/electric/sewer, Loop D) plus standard sites, yurts and cabins; RVs to ~40-50 ft. Reservations via ActiveMONTGOMERY. Campground loops sit on wooded uplands, not on the water. RV Life 4.5★, price $$$ (3/4). --Claude

name: Louise F. Cosca Regional Park Campground
note: Prince George's County / M-NCPPC (Louise F. Cosca Regional Park), Clinton MD. 25 sites - all with water, 5 with electric; RVs to ~45 ft; dump station. Reservations via the county PARKS DIRECT system. Campground sits on wooded high ground set back from Lake Cosca (not on the water). RV Life 3.5★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Watkins Regional Park Campground
note: Prince George's County / M-NCPPC (Watkins Regional Park), Upper Marlboro MD. 30 individual sites + group sites, no hookups; self-contained RVs and trailers explicitly permitted per the PG Parks camping brochure (some review sites wrongly say 'no RVs'). Hot showers. Individual sites are first-come, first-served (group sites reserved). Wooded; the park's pond and creek are away from the campground (not on the water). RV Life unrated, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: 1889 Park
note: Adams & Croyle Township Recreation Authority (municipal joint authority), South Fork PA, next to the Johnstown Flood National Memorial. ~9-10 level RV hookup sites (30/50-amp, water/sewer) plus tent sites. Reservations by phone/email. Not waterfront (the historic Lake Conemaugh basin is drained). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Duman Lake County Park
note: Cambria County's only county park, near Ebensburg PA. 6 back-in RV sites (30-amp electric + water, rigs to ~40 ft), bathhouse; season ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Reservations. Adjoins 19-acre Duman Lake (PA Fish & Boat) but the sites are wooded and set back from the water. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Hibernia County Park
note: Chester County park (900+ acres) near Coatesville PA. RV-capable only at Fiddler's Loop sites #3/#17/#18 (RVs to ~28 ft; a 23-ft rig fits); no hookups, no dump station. Reservations online/phone or first-come self-registration; open weekends Apr through mid-Nov. Chambers Lake and Brandywine Creek are in the park but the RV-capable sites are wooded/inland. RV Life unrated, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Larnard-Hornbrook County Park
note: Bradford County park on the Susquehanna River near Ulster PA. 40 RV sites (water+electric) plus a tent area, boat launch, dump station, showers; season ~May-mid-Oct. Some RV sites sit directly on the riverbank, though most riverfront sites are held by seasonal campers (limited transient availability). RV Life unrated, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Sunfish Pond County Park
note: Bradford County park atop Barclay Mountain around 30-acre Sunfish Pond, near Canton PA (Leroy Twp). 38 electric RV sites plus ~12 tent sites and primitive cabins; dump station, showers, camp store; seasonal (first Sat May-second Sat Oct). A few RV sites sit along the pond. RV Life unrated, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Two Mile Run County Park
note: Venango County park (2,695 acres) on 144-acre Justus Lake near Franklin PA. Drive-in RV camping: 12 full-hookup + 28 electric sites (30/50-amp), modern showers, dump station, camp host. The lakeside 'water's edge' sites (loops E/F) are walk-in/primitive tent sites; the RV loops are set back east of the lake, so waterfront none. Reservations via the park office. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Big Country Campgrounds
note: Private family campground near Brookville (Jefferson Co). ~100 RV sites, full hookups/30-amp, 10 pull-thru, RVs to ~40 ft; open ~mid-Apr to mid-Dec. Reservations. Wooded hills, not waterfront. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Burnt Cabins Grist Mill & Family Campground
note: Private family campground at a historic working grist mill near Burnt Cabins (Fulton Co), 5 mi from PA Turnpike Exit 180. ~44 sites incl. 34 full-hookup gravel, big-rig friendly. Borders Little Aughwick Creek and a mill pond, but RV-site water frontage not confirmed. RV Life unrated, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Canyon Country Campground
note: Private campground near Wellsboro (Tioga Co), close to the PA Grand Canyon / Pine Creek Rail Trail. 50+ gravel sites, mostly full hookups, big-rig friendly, dump station; seasonal ~Apr 15-Oct 31. No on-site water. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Colwell's Campground
note: Private campground near Knox (Clarion Co), adjacent to the former Sportsman's Speedway. Pull-thru RV sites, 30/50-amp, sewer/dump, open year-round; call for reservations. Not waterfront. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Crawford's Camping Park
note: Private family campground near Darlington (Beaver Co), close to the PA Turnpike/Ohio line. Full hookups, 50-amp; nightly/monthly/seasonal, open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Has an on-site fishing pond (amenity; RV sites not confirmed pond-front). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Fox Den Acres
note: Large private campground (~350 sites) near New Stanton (Westmoreland Co), 2 mi off PA Turnpike Exit 75. Big-rig friendly, 105 pull-thrus, full hookups, RVs to ~75 ft; transient + seasonal. Some sites overlook the on-site lake (RV sites near it not confirmed on-water). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Frey's Campground
note: Private family campground on the West Branch Susquehanna River at Watsontown (Northumberland Co). 63 large sites with water/sewer/electric; shore access for kayaks/canoes. RV sites along the river corridor with river views (on-bank siting not confirmed). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Glendale Valley Campground
note: Private campground at Fallentimber (Cambria Co), ~2 mi from Glendale Lake/Prince Gallitzin SP. All full-hookup, 30/50-amp, RVs to ~42 ft; nightly via Campspot. Some RV sites sit creekside along an on-site creek. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Hare Creek Campground
note: Private year-round campground at Corry (Erie Co). All full-hookup, 12 pull-thrus, 20/30/50-amp, handles large rigs. Adjacent to Hare Creek and an on-site pond, but the creekside sites are tent/primitive (RV sites not on water). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Hickory Hollow Campground
note: Private RV/tent/cabin resort (75 acres) near Rockwood (Somerset Co). Full hookups, 50-amp, wide level sites, big-rig friendly. Has a 5-acre fishing lake, but the lakeside units are covered-wagon rentals (RV sites not on the water). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Lake Bonin Campground
note: Private family campground (100 acres) around private Lake Bonin near Rome (Bradford Co); camp store and roller rink. Full hookups, 30/50-amp; daily/weekly/seasonal. Camping loops ring the lake with lake views (shoreline RV siting not fully confirmed). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Lauder Family Campground
note: Small private family campground (~14 sites) at Frenchville (Clearfield Co); bookable nightly (also on Hipcamp). Back-in RV sites, 30/50-amp electric, free fill/dump, new bathhouse. Has an upper fishing pond (RV sites not pond-front). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Meadow Lake Campground
note: Private family campground (~180 sites) near Jamestown (Mercer Co); set back ~2 mi from Pymatuning Lake (NOT on it). Full hookups, 50-amp, RVs to ~50 ft; seasonal ~Apr 15-Oct 15, nightly available. Has a small on-site lake/pond (RV-site frontage unconfirmed). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Miller's Campground
note: Private family campground near New Alexandria (Westmoreland Co), 3 min from Keystone State Park. Many full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp, dump station; nightly/weekly/seasonal. No on-site water body. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Mitchell Lake Campgrounds
note: Small private full-hookup RV park (~11-12 sites) near Cambridge Springs (Crawford Co), sitting just above 30-acre Mitchell Lake. 30/50-amp, RVs to ~40 ft; daily/weekly/monthly, no tents, open ~May-Oct. Sites overlook the lake from above (not on the shoreline). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Moyer's Grove Campground
note: Private family campground (~147 sites) at Wapwallopen (Luzerne Co); pool, mini-golf, store, boat rentals. Full/partial hookups, 20/30/50-amp, RVs to ~40 ft (note a 14'2" covered-bridge height clearance on access). RV sites not confirmed on water. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Oil Creek Family Campground
note: Private family campground (54 wooded acres, ~97 sites) near Titusville (Crawford Co); set back from Oil Creek itself. Big-rig friendly, 47 pull-thrus, 20/30/50-amp; open ~mid-Apr-Nov 1. Not waterfront. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Otter Creek Campground
note: Private campground (89 sites) on PPL Susquehanna Riverlands preserve land at Airville (York Co), run by a private operator. 50+ gravel W/E sites, 30/50-amp; open Apr-Oct. Interior roads narrow/rutted but passable for a 23-ft rig. Susquehanna River frontage is day-use/boat-launch, not camping - RV sites set back. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Pequea Creek Campground
note: Private campground on PPL preserve land at Pequea (Lancaster Co), run by a private operator. ~30 travel-trailer sites + tents, RVs to ~40 ft, 30/50-amp; open ~mid-Apr-Oct. Advertises creekside sites backing onto Pequea Creek (some RV/trailer sites on the creek). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: RV Village Camping Resort
note: Private RV resort (~296 sites) near Mercer (Mercer Co), marketed for quick overnight stays off I-79/I-80 plus seasonal. Full hookups, pull-thrus. On-site fishing pond + pool (RV sites not pond-front). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Riverside Acres Campground
note: Private campground (41 sites) on the Susquehanna River at Towanda (Bradford Co); ~650 ft of river frontage. 12 full-hookup + 24 electric/water sites, big-rig friendly, RVs to ~40 ft. Some electric RV sites sit directly on the riverbank. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Rock Mountain Bible Camp
note: Christian camp near Susquehanna (Susquehanna Co) that runs a public family campground open to all (online nightly booking, non-discrimination policy). Numbered W/E sites, some sewer, RVs to ~40 ft; seasonal ~May 15-Oct 12. Note: no-alcohol and conduct standards apply. On-site lake (RV sites not on it). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Shady Rest Campground
note: Private campground (35 upgraded sites) at South Gibson (Susquehanna Co), 3 mi from I-81 Exit 211. Level gravel W/S/30-50-amp sites, RVs to ~40 ft; welcomes overnighters. Tunkhannock Creek nearby but creekside sites are primitive/tent (RV sites not on water). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Soaring Eagle Campground
note: Private family campground on the Upper Delaware River at Equinunk (Wayne Co), just above Kellam's Bridge. Partial-hookup short-term RV sites (W/E) + full-hookup seasonal; no weekday minimums. RV sites adjacent to the Delaware River. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Squarewood Acres Campground
note: Private campground (90 sites + 2 cabins, formerly Sheshequin) at Trout Run (Lycoming Co); open Apr-Oct. 30/50-amp sites that accommodate any size RV, dump station, bathhouse. On a creek (fishing/tubing) but RV-site creek frontage not confirmed. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Stony Mountain Family Campground
note: Private family campground (57 acres, 85 sites) near Tunkhannock (Wyoming Co) with a 12-acre fishing lake. 20/30/50-amp, big-rig friendly, RVs to ~40 ft; nightly via Campspot/Hipcamp, open May 1-Oct 15. Overnight RV sites are by the lake or the swimming pond. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Whispering Winds Campground
note: Private campground & cabins (20 acres) at Sheffield (Warren Co), bordering the Allegheny National Forest. RV sites mostly full-hookup, 30-amp standard/50-amp on request. A creek runs through the property (RV-site frontage not confirmed). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: White's Haven Campground
note: Private campground & cabins near Clarington (Jefferson Co), close to Cook Forest SP/Clarion River. W/E sites, 30/50-amp, pull-thru gravel, RVs to ~60 ft; open year-round (Passport America). On-site fishing pond is a separate amenity; Clarion River is off-site. Not waterfront. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Woodhaven Acres Campground
note: Private campground & cabins (25 sites) near Bradford (McKean Co), within/near the Allegheny National Forest on Route 59. All full-hookup, 6 pull-thrus, pool; open May 1-Oct 31. Boat launches/beach ~8 mi away (not on-site). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Woodland Park
note: Private family-owned campground (est. 1960, 200 sites) near Ebensburg (Cambria Co); 'Inc.' is just the corporate form - open to all, nightly/monthly/seasonal, open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Full hookups, 8 pull-thrus, RVs to ~50 ft. Pool only, not waterfront. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Quemahoning Family Recreation Area
note: Cambria Somerset Authority (municipal water authority) recreation area on the 900-acre Quemahoning Reservoir near Hollsopple (Somerset Co); the only public access to the lake, day-to-day run by Summer's Best Two Weeks, open to the public. 28 RV sites (30-amp, some 50-amp), firm pads, dump/water nearby. RV sites have good lake views (tent sites are on the shoreline). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Beckley Exhibition Coal Mine Campground
note: City of Beckley campground in New River Park (run via the Exhibition Coal Mine). 17 paved back-in sites, full hookups (50-amp/water/sewer); self-contained units only (no on-site restrooms/showers). Reservations; open ~Apr 1-Nov 1. Inland city park, not waterfront. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Great Bend Campground
note: City of Ravenswood (Board of Parks & Rec) campground on the Ohio River near the Sandy Creek confluence. 18 RV sites (13 premium full-hookup 30-80 ft, 5 standard 30-amp/28 ft) plus boat docks; open year-round, reservations. Some RV sites sit directly along the river. RV Life 4★, price $$$ (3/4). --Claude

name: Krodel Park Campground
note: City of Point Pleasant municipal park around a 22-acre stocked lake. ~64 full-hookup RV sites (back-in + pull-thru, concrete) ringing the lakeshore plus primitive tent spots; open ~Apr 1-Oct 31 (14 winter sites). Reservations. Many RV sites front the lake. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: St. Albans Roadside Park
note: City of St. Albans Parks & Rec; small RV-only park (~3-5 electric sites, dump station) on the Kanawha River. First-come, first-served, free (donations encouraged), 2-night max per ~14 days. Tight - fits rigs under ~39 ft. Some sites sit right on the river; adjacent to busy MacCorkle Ave (road noise). RV Life 4★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Brooke Hills Park
note: Brooke County park (750 acres) near Wellsburg. ~29 RV sites (50-ft pull-thrus + back-ins, full/partial hookups) plus primitive tent camping; reservations via CampLife. Has catch-and-release fishing ponds but RV sites are not confirmed pond-front. (Coords are park-level - refine to the RV loop if needed.) RV Life 3.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Grand Vue Park
note: Marshall County Park & Recreation Board park near Moundsville, on the former par-3 golf course. 40 back-in RV sites, full hookups (50-amp/water/sewer), 65-ft pads; reservations via CampLife. Ridge-top setting overlooking the Ohio Valley - not on water. RV Life 4.5★, price $$$ (3/4). --Claude

name: Kitty Wilson Campground
note: Calhoun County Park near Mt. Zion (south of Grantsville). 8 sites with electric (30/20/15-amp), gravel pads; restrooms/showers in the Community Building. Seasonal (~Memorial Day-Labor Day), advance arrangement; designated Dark Sky site. Park has ponds but RV-site water frontage not confirmed. RV Life 4★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Lone Pine Campground (Doddridge County Park)
note: Doddridge County Park near West Union, by the North Bend Rail Trail. 24 RV slips (8 full-hookup asphalt 50/30-amp, 16 gravel water/electric, varying lengths) plus tent sites and yurts; reservations via Campspot. Borders Meathouse Fork Creek, but the creekside sites are the wooded tent sites (RV slips set back). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Beaver Lakes Campground
note: Private campground on a ~250-acre reclaimed property near Calvin (Nicholas Co), close to New River Gorge/Summersville Lake. Full-hookup back-in RV sites (15/20/30/50-amp), RVs to ~45 ft; longish gravel access road (in good shape). Has scattered ponds but RV sites not confirmed pond-front. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Big Earl's Campground
note: Family-run campground at Julian (Boone Co), a Hatfield-McCoy/Ivy Branch ATV trail-access campground. 14 RV sites (water + 20/30/50-amp, mix of full-hookup and electric/water) plus cabins and tent field; open year-round, reservations by phone/text. Near the Big Coal River but RV sites not confirmed on water. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Blue Heron Landing
note: Private RV campground on Mill Creek at Cottageville (Jackson Co). 67 full-hookup lots (30/50-amp), kayak/canoe rental, open to all with reservations. The creek-side sites are the tent sites; RV lots are not confirmed on the water. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Boyer Station Campground
note: Private campground + motel at Arbovale (Pocahontas Co), near Cass Scenic Railroad/Green Bank. 80 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp) plus primitive/tent sites and motel rooms, dump station, showers; open year-round. Inland mountain valley, not waterfront. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Bradley Campground
note: Small private roadside campground on Robert C. Byrd Dr at Bradley (Raleigh Co, near Beckley). RV-usable drive-in sites (hookup/site details lightly documented - verify by phone). Not waterfront. Distinct from JG Bradley Campground in Clay County. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Camp Morning Wood
note: Private family RV campground (gated, ~15 sites on 20 wooded acres) along the Middle Fork River between Buckhannon and Elkins (Uppershur/Randolph Co); on-site rafting/kayaking/tubing. Full hookups, 50-amp; nightly bookings (Hipcamp). Despite the joke name it's a normal public campground. RV sites near/overlooking the river (on-bank siting not confirmed). RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Creekside Camping
note: Family-owned campground near Triadelphia (Ohio Co, near Wheeling). 14 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp); pool/hot tub. Nearly every site fronts the on-site creek (with a small waterfall) - creekfront RV sites confirmed. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Elk River Camp and RV
note: Private riverfront RV park on the Elk River at Gassaway (Braxton Co), off I-79 Exit 62. ~24 full-hookup sites plus W/E and electric-only, 30/50-amp, RVs to ~50 ft, on-site boat launch (Elk River Water Trail). Some RV sites sit directly on the river. (Horse-friendly but not an equestrian campground.) RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Lakeview Campground
note: Private RV-only campground (~18 sites, full hookups, 50-amp, big-rig friendly) near Harrisville (Ritchie Co); open ~May-Sep. On-site fishing pond; sites overlook the small on-site pond (not North Bend Lake, which is ~2.5 mi away). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Mountain Top RV Park & Cabins
note: Private RV park & cabins on a 140-acre mountain-top property ~1.5 mi from the Summersville Lake boat launch (Nicholas Co). Full-hookup sites (30/50-amp) plus premium wooded and primitive sites and cabins. The lake is a nearby attraction, not at the sites. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Mud Pit ATV Resort
note: Private ATV/RV resort on the Bluestone River off the Pocahontas (Hatfield-McCoy) trail near Bluefield (Mercer Co). 13 large RV sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer) plus cabins, store/fuel; public nightly bookings. RV sites are riverfront on the Bluestone River. RV Life 5★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: New River Campground
note: Private campground on the New River (just above the Gauley confluence) at Gauley Bridge (Fayette Co), on the Midland Trail/Rt 60. 39 full-hookup sites (big-rig to ~60 ft) plus 13 rustic tent-only sites; approach road is steep/twisty but OK for a 23-ft rig. Several full-hookup RV sites sit right on the river. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Recreational Rentals and Sales Park
note: Private RV-only park (11 full-hookup sites, 20/50-amp, big-rig to ~52 ft) at Parsons (Tucker Co), near the Allegheny Trail/Cheat River. Public, ~$20/day; year-round. Near the Cheat River but RV sites not confirmed on the bank. RV Life 4.5★, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Rest Rite Campground
note: Private campground near the entrance to Blackwater Falls State Park at Davis (Tucker Co). Spacious full-hookup RV sites (incl. pull-thrus) plus tent sites; public nightly. Wooded lots near the park, not on the water. RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Rippling Waters Campground
note: Church-affiliated nonprofit campground (open to the public) on a 7-acre stocked lake near Kenna (Jackson Co), ~4 mi up a narrow road off I-77 Exit 116. 27 full-hookup RV sites (20/30/50-amp), big-rig accessible; some sites sit on the lake. Note: no alcohol/smoking on grounds. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Robert Newlon Airpark RV Park
note: Private public RV park beside a working grass airstrip at Lesage/Huntington (Cabell Co). 30 full-hookup pull-thru sites (30/50-amp, to ~79 ft), dump station, bathhouse, laundry; low-cost overnight stop. Ohio River ~0.5 mi away, not at the sites. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Ruby Lake Campground
note: Small family-run campground (~20 sites) around a pond ('Ruby Lake') at Sandyville (Jackson Co). Full-hookup pull-thru sites, 30/50-amp, RVs to ~55 ft; first-come, first-served, open year-round. A few sites sit directly on the pond. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Saylwinds
note: Private 28-acre campground near Elkins (Randolph Co), self-check-in (no office), online booking via Campspot. 13 full-hookup RV sites (30/50-amp) plus partial/primitive; in the National Radio Quiet Zone. Borders Chenoweth Creek but RV-site frontage not confirmed. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Starry Fields RV Park
note: Private family-owned RV park (22 full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp) near New Cumberland (Hancock Co), ~2.4 mi from Tomlinson Run State Park. Open field setting, not waterfront. No on-site restroom/shower (self-contained). RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Sunny Glen Farms RV Park
note: Private family/pet-friendly RV park near Salem (Harrison Co); open all year, reservations, takes nightly transients. 30/50-amp, sewer, laundry. Not waterfront. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: The Dock Campground
note: Private RV campground/marina at St. Marys (Pleasants Co) where Middle Island Creek meets the Ohio River. 57 cement-pad full-hookup sites (30/50-amp), big-rig friendly, direct river/marina docking. RV sites adjacent to the water. Reserve via Facebook Messenger; favors longer stays. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Travel Trailer Center RV Park
note: Private campground (~32 grass sites; ~25 electric+water, 7 electric-only, 30-amp; dump station) on the Little Kanawha River at Parkersburg (Wood Co). RV sites sit right along the river. First-come, first-served, honor-system payment. Some highway noise. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Triple L Campground
note: Private campground (16 RV/tent sites, full hookups, city water, 50-amp, level gravel) near Salem (Harrison Co), close to UHC/VA hospital. Daily/weekly/monthly - welcomes short-term travelers. Near a rail-trail, not waterfront. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Tymber Park RV Campground
note: Private RV park (44 sites, 30/50-amp, city water, full sewer, shower house, laundry) at Clarksburg (Harrison Co). Oriented to long-term/worker stays but welcomes travelers passing through; bookable nightly via Campspot. Not waterfront. RV Life 4.5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: West Virginia State Fairgrounds
note: State Fair of WV (nonprofit) fairgrounds at Fairlea (Greenbrier Co), 3 mi off I-64. Hundreds of sites (175+ full-hookup, big-rig friendly) plus partial/electric/dry. Pass-through camping bookable outside fair dates; seasonal (~closed Nov-Mar). Not waterfront. Rates/availability change during the fair and events. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Lake Stephens Campground
note: Raleigh County Parks & Recreation Authority park on 272-acre Lake Stephens (2,300-acre county park) near Beckley. 100 full-hookup pull-thru RV sites (30/50-amp) plus a separate tent campground, cabins, marina, beach; open May 1-Oct 31, reservations via Campspot. The RV loop is set back with lake views (lake access via marina/beach); RV sites tight/treeless. RV Life 4★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: East Fork Campground
note: Private campground ('East Fork Campground and Horse Hotel') on the East Fork of the Greenbrier River at Durbin (Pocahontas Co); the operator notes horses are kept away from the campground. Full-hookup (30-amp) RV sites with pull-thrus plus tent sites, cabin, lodge and group area; open ~Apr-Oct, ~$56/night. Tent sites line the riverbank; RV sites are mixed (some riverfront, some set back). RV Life 5★, price $$ (2/4). --Claude

name: Atwood Lake Park
note: Muskingum Watershed Conservancy District (regional authority). 530+ sites, electric & full-hookup loops; Area H has 142 lakefront full-hookup RV sites w/ concrete pads. Seasonal Apr-Oct, online reservations. RV Life 5*/$$$. --Claude

name: Ottawa Metro Park
note: Johnny Appleseed Metropolitan Park District. 30 sites, 8 pull-throughs, water/electric (50-amp), dump station, showers. Open mid-Apr to mid-Oct. First-come-first-served, self-registration (no reservations). RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Mercer County Fairgrounds
note: Mercer County Agricultural Society. Public seasonal RV camping May-Oct (not fair-only): 15 full-hookup, 105 electric, 30 electric/water, 30/50-amp. Reservations via Firefly. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ross County Fairgrounds
note: Ross County Agricultural Society. Full-hookup 30/50-amp RV sites; ~$20/night non-fair camping (also a Harvest Hosts host), confirming transient use outside the fair. Call ahead. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Miami Whitewater Forest Campground
note: Great Parks of Hamilton County (park district). 46 sites, 30-amp electric, renovated shower house, dump station; some sites tight for big rigs. Online reservations. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: White Star Park Campground
note: Sandusky County Park District. 48 water/electric sites (50/30-amp, no sewer), shower house. Open Apr 15-Nov 1, online reservations only (no walk-ins). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Nimisila Reservoir Metro Park Campground
note: Summit Metro Parks (park district). ~29 sites, ~6-18 electric, dump station; NO drinking water, vault toilets only. On Nimisila Reservoir, reservoir access from many sites. Reserve America; closed Jan-Feb. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Darke County Fairgrounds
note: Darke County Agricultural Society. ~600 hookups (200 full-hookup 20/30/50-amp + water/electric), 2 dump stations. Open Apr-Oct (closed Aug for fair). First-come-first-served, no reservations ($30/night). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Caesar Ford Park Campground
note: Greene County Parks & Trails. 12 Class-A full-hookup pull-through sites (water, 30/50-amp, sewer) for large RVs; shower house, dump station. Opened 2024; reservations required. Connects to equestrian trails but is a general RV campground. RV Life 5*/$$$. --Claude

name: Van Wert County Fairgrounds
note: Van Wert County Agricultural Society. 18 paved full/partial-hookup sites (30-amp), big-rig friendly, dump station. Public seasonal camping ~Apr-Nov outside fair events. Reservations by phone. RV Life 3*/$$. --Claude

name: Pleasant Hill Lake Park
note: Muskingum Watershed Conservancy District. ~380 sites, 50-amp electric + full-hookup big-rig sites (Area F concrete pads); sites range from lakefront to wooded. Reservable. Area E closed for construction to 2027. RV Life 3.5*/$$$. --Claude

name: Miami County Fairgrounds
note: Miami County Agricultural Society. Year-round public overnight RV camping except during the August fair. ~$15 dry / $30 electric, dump station, electric stands. RV Life 3*/$$. --Claude

name: Piedmont Marina & Campground
note: Muskingum Watershed Conservancy District. ~80 sites on Piedmont Lake, full-hookup concrete pads + 50-amp, premium lakefront sites, RVs to ~35 ft. Reservable. RV Life 4.5*/$$$. --Claude

name: Village Green Campground
note: Village of North Kingsville municipal campground. 72 sites, 20-amp electric + water (no sewer; dump across street), max ~50 ft, pull-thrus, pool. Open ~Apr 30-Oct 15. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sally Buffalo Park Campground
note: Village of Cadiz municipal park. 200-300+ level sites, 30-amp electric + water, dump station, four lakes, open year-round. Reservable. RV Life 2.5*/$ (cheap public park). --Claude

name: Perry Township Park
note: Perry Township (Lake County) park on Lake Erie. 30 RV sites w/ 30-amp electric + showers; sites on the lakefront bluff have Erie views. Day/week/month permits, online via Campspot. Open ~May-Nov; many seasonal but transient available. RV Life 4*/$$$. --Claude

name: Friendship Park (Pugliese Campground)
note: Jefferson County's Friendship Park. Drive-in reservable RV sites, 30-amp electric + water, $20-30/night, at the park's 89-acre lake. Offers horseback trails but is a general campground. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Gallia County Junior Fairgrounds
note: Gallia County Agricultural Society. Year-round public RV camping, 200 full-hookup (30/50-amp) sites, online reservations (Firefly); $40/night Apr-Oct, $60 Nov-Mar; no tents. Catch-and-release pond on site. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Washington County Fair Park
note: Washington County Agricultural Society. Year-round public RV camping, full hookups (30/50-amp), asphalt sites, 12 pull-thrus; FCFS honor system (pay at Fair Board office), water off Nov-Apr. RV Life 2.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Clark County Fairgrounds
note: Clark County Agricultural Society. Year-round public RV camping, 188 sites (30/50-amp, water, 2 dump stations), $30/night; may be unavailable on special-event dates (call ahead). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Greene County Expo Center Campground
note: Greene County Agricultural Society (Xenia). RV camping year-round except fair & Hamvention, 100+ sites (30/50-amp, water, 2 dump), 20x50 lots, Firefly reservations. RV Life 2.5*/$$$. --Claude

name: Jackson County Fairgrounds
note: Jackson County Agricultural Society (Wellston). Year-round full-hookup RV camping, ~90 sites, $25/night, FCFS (no reservations for hookups); non-hookup overflow available. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Pastime Park Campground
note: Village of Plain City municipal campground. 56 water/electric sites (38 grass, 18 gravel pads 70x15 ft, big-rig capable). Open Apr-Oct, 30-day max stay. Reservable via Campspot. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Coshocton Lake Park Campground
note: Coshocton Park District. 69 back-in gravel sites, electric-only (30-amp; 2 sites 50-amp), no full hookups, dump station; part of Coshocton Lake Park Recreational Complex. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Meadowbrook Park
note: Hopewell Township (Seneca County). 38 weekend/transient + 177 seasonal sites, electric + water, RVs to ~40 ft; historic Redwood Ballroom on site. Open mid-Apr to Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. (Address Bascom, OH.) --Claude

name: Winklepleck Grove Campground
note: Village of Sugarcreek municipal grove. Rustic: 15-amp electric only, no dump/showers, non-flush toilets, $25/night, open May-Sept; open grassy sites, hosts RV rallies. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Scenic Hills RV Park
note: 112 full-hookup big-rig sites on 30 acres in Amish country. Open Apr 1-Nov 1, takes reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: River Trail Crossing
note: 31 full-hookup sites on the Clear Fork River near Mohican SP, big-rig friendly w/ pull-throughs; riverfront RV sites confirmed by guest reviews. Adjacent B&O bike trail. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Baylor Beach Park
note: Family-owned (since 1954) full-hookup big-rig campground + water park. Open May-Oct. On-site swim/paddle lakes are recreation features, not RV waterfront. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: National Road Campground
note: 80 water/electric/sewer sites, accommodates up to 75 ft, 2 stocked fishing ponds, splash pad. Online + phone reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Sunny's Campground
note: 800 sites/115 acres/5 lakes; mostly seasonal but designated nightly/weekend lots exist, RVs to 75 ft. Open ~late Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ashland RV Park
note: 28 gravel sites on the Muskingum River w/ boat ramp, daily/weekly/monthly. Riverfront sites are seasonal; transient sites are uphill near road. Cash/check only. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Carthage Gap RV Park
note: 135 full-hookup big-rig sites on 31 acres in SE Ohio, 2 spring-fed ponds (swim/fish). Open May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (9.1/10). --Claude

name: Enon Beach Campground
note: 150 sites (44 full-hookup, 32 pull-thru, 20 tent), big-rig friendly, swim/paddle lakes. Family-oriented (not nudist). Facebook is primary web presence. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Poor Farmer's Campground
note: 500+ water/electric sites, ~$28/night, creek + fishing pond; co-located RV dealership. Online booking via Campspot. Large (some seasonal) but confirmed transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ridge Ranch Campground
note: 76 acres w/ 17-acre swim lake & beach, full-hookup pull-throughs. Open May-Oct, online nightly bookings (GatherGrounds Resorts; reservation-based, not membership). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Fire Lake Campground
note: 96 full-hookup big-rig sites (to 40 ft) around an 8-acre swim/fish lake; lakefront + lakeview sites, boat ramp. Open May-Oct, takes overnight bookings. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Cutty's Sunset Camping Resort
note: Family campground (est. 1971, ~500 sites), mix of seasonal + overnight; level gravel sites (some tight). Nightly bookings via RoverPass. Season ~May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kenisee Grand River Campground
note: ~25 full-hookup 50-amp pull-thru sites overlooking a small lake; private fishing lake. RV-only (no tent/cabin), nightly bookings. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Amish Country Campsites
note: Mennonite-run, ~50 gravel sites, electric+water, dump station, max RV ~40 ft (sites tight). No standalone website (directory/booking listings). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Town & Country Camp Resort
note: 170 acres off I-71; 50 full-hookup + 30 water/electric sites, pull-thru & back-in, stocked fishing lake, pool. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tree Haven Campground
note: Family campground (est. 1973) NE of Columbus; 130 sites (50 tent), 20 pull-thrus, max RV ~28 ft. Season May-Nov. No standalone website (county tourism/booking listings). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Wood's Tall Timber Lake Resort
note: 157 acres; daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal camping, 40 pull-thrus, concrete pads, big rigs. 7-acre swim lake, ziplines, mini-golf. Season Apr-Nov. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Bay Shore Family Campground
note: ~300 sites on Pymatuning Reservoir, big rigs to 60 ft, boat dock/launch; dedicated Lake Front RV sites on the water. Mostly seasonal but takes daily/nightly. Facebook-only web presence. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Huron River Valley Resort
note: ~200 sites (overnight + seasonal), marina w/ Lake Erie access, ~1 mi Huron River frontage; overnight $65/night. Riverfront overnight-site relationship unconfirmed. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Arrowhead Lake RV Park & Campground
note: 115 sites (49 full-hookup), swimming + fishing ponds (amenity, not RV-site waterfront). Season May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Adventure Bound - Pleasant View
note: Public family resort off I-75 near Findlay; full-hookup RV + tent + cabins, nightly from $38, online booking. Pool, aqua park on pond, mini-golf. Not a membership park. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Lancaster Camp Ground
note: Historic (1878) Christian camp-meeting nonprofit operating a public RV park: 24 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp), nightly/weekly/monthly, dump station; alcohol-free. Near Hocking Hills. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Flying Finn Family Campground
note: ~35 sites, big-rig friendly, full/partial hookups (30/50-amp), 13-acre fishing pond, pool. Camper/trailer only (no tents). Family-owned since 1979. Open May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Long Lake Park
note: 286 full-hookup sites (mostly 50-amp) next to a 60-acre spring-fed lake; mix of seasonal + transient (overnight sites limited, reserve early). Listed for sale 2024-25 - confirm operating. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Back 40 Campground
note: 82 sites (32 overnight + seasonals), 30-amp electric+water, gravel/cement back-in pads, big rigs OK; two ponds (fishing + swimming). Near Indian Lake. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Foxfire Campground
note: ~150 full/partial-hookup sites, big-rig friendly to 50 ft, overnight + seasonal; bordered by Brokensword Creek (RV sites not creekside). Open May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lively Lady Marina & Campground
note: ~200 sites (some seasonal full-timers) w/ marina + boat launch on the Ohio River, full hookups 30/50-amp; ~1 mi from Maysville KY. Facebook-only web presence. RV Life 5*/$$ (10/10). --Claude

name: Cherokee Park Family Campground
note: 120 sites, 30/50-amp electric+water, pull-throughs, dump station; on small private lakes/ponds, <1 mi to Mogadore Reservoir. Facebook/Good Sam web presence. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Loveberry's Funny Farm Campground
note: 63 acres, pull-through 30/50-amp full-hookup sites, big-rig friendly, cabins + tent sites, fishing/swim pond. Family-run. Open ~Apr-Oct, books via CampLife. OH/MI border off I-80/90. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lazy Village Campground
note: ~63-104 sites on a former racetrack off US-52, full hookups 30/50-amp, big rigs to ~72 ft, gravel pads. Near Shawnee State Forest. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (9.3/10). --Claude

name: Chaparral Family Campground
note: 80 acres, 100+ sites (primitive to full hookup), big-rig friendly pull-throughs, 5-acre lake w/ boat rentals. Open May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Toodik
note: 93 acres, ~223 sites, RVs to 45 ft. Main Hill full-hookup + River Edge water/electric RV sites fronting the Lake Fork (Mohican) branch. Canoe livery, pool, mini-golf. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Oak Crest Campground
note: ~136 sites (34 full hookup, 102 elec/water), 50 pull-throughs, RVs to ~35 ft, heated pool, 7-acre stocked lake. Open Apr 15-Oct 31. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Timberwoods Campground
note: ~158 sites, full hookup 30/50-amp, RVs to 60 ft, pull-throughs, pool + fishing pond. Seasonal Apr-Oct, takes short-term. New owners; mixed mgmt reviews. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Hickory Grove Lake
note: ~38 acres, full-hookup standard & premium RV sites, big-rig pull-throughs, 9-acre stocked lake, beach, pool, dog park. Borders Marion Tallgrass Trail. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: A1-Twin Valley Campground
note: 107 gravel sites, full/partial hookups, big-rig friendly to 40 ft (est. 1980); pool, stocked fishing pond. Public/transient. Facebook-only web presence. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Kokosing Valley Camp & Canoe
note: ~150 sites, water/electric/sewer, big-rig friendly to 50 ft, canoe livery on the State Scenic Kokosing River, pool. Riverfront sites reserved for full-season campers (not transient). Season May-Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Shady Trails Family Campground
note: ~130 sites, 30/50-amp full hookups, pull-throughs to ~85 ft, near Rocky Fork Lake. Mostly seasonal but accepts transient nightly. No standalone website (Good Sam/Campendium). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Atwood Lake Country Campground
note: Full-hookup RV sites (20/30/50-amp), large rigs, pool, catch-and-release fishing pond; inland near (not on) Atwood Lake. Firefly online reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Shadow Lake RV Park & Campground
note: 96 acres w/ lake + 3 ponds, ~100 full-hookup big-rig sites, pool, mini-golf, pickleball; some sites have lake views. Open year-round; transient + long-term. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Campers Grove RV Park
note: ~30 sites, pet-friendly, tenting, buddy sites, playground; off I-70 near Dillon Lake & Hopewell Culture NHP. Season Apr-Oct, $21/night, reservations by phone. No official website (directory listings). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Hidden Hills Campground
note: ~62 sites, full hookups 20/30/50-amp, pull-throughs, RVs to 60 ft, dump station, playground. Seasonal mid-Apr to Oct; some full-time residents but transient-bookable. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Nelson Ledges Quarry Park
note: 250-acre quarry swim/scuba/festival park w/ a designated ~60-site RV camping area + dump station, limited FCFS electric, no water hookups. Non-festival camping is walk-up/first-come. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Indian Trail Campground
note: 169 sites (106 full hookup, 60 water/electric, 40 primitive), 30/50-amp, pool + 8-acre lake; max RV ~48 ft. Open late Apr-early Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Clinton Lake Camping
note: ~200 sites (160 water/electric 30-amp, 40 tent, 10 pull-throughs), pool/clubhouse, fishing + swim lakes. Nightly + seasonal. Open May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: River Bend Family Campground
note: 240 full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp, pool + pond, on the Olentangy/Whetstone River (riverfront RV sites unconfirmed). Nightly + seasonal. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Sun Valley Family Campground
note: 242 water/electric sites, pool + fishing pond (since 1987), ~9 mi E of Lima. Open Apr 15-Oct 31. Primary web presence Facebook/ohiocamper. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: RiverView Kampground
note: 53 full-hookup sites + marina/boat ramp, pool, on the Muskingum River; riverfront/marina sites are seasonal-only (daily campers get inland sites). Facebook web presence. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Schaun Acres Campground
note: 130 gravel-pad sites, pool + 2 fishing ponds; mostly seasonal but offers nightly electric ($35) + primitive. Open May-Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Arrow Point Campground
