note: Private (Putney, near Harlan); family-owned (Foster family) - despite the name, not a county park. 19 RV/tent sites + cabins at the Black Mountain Adventure Area off-road trailhead; bathhouses; nightly/weekly/monthly. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hopewell RV Park
note: Private (Drakesboro, Muhlenberg Co). 40 full-hookup sites (city water/sewer, upgraded electric), WiFi; level large pads, big-rig friendly. Mixed clientele - vacation overnighters plus some longer-term contract workers (TVA Paradise / Logan Aluminum). RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Timeless Days Campground
note: Private (Lucas), 0.5 mi from Barren River Lake SP (formerly Spring Hill RV Park, new management). 40 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50A), nightly to seasonal; quiet, helpful owner. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: County Line Campground & Storage
note: Private (Dover, ~8 mi W of Maysville on the AA Highway). 20 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water/sewer), DSL; also offers RV/boat storage. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Paintsville Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Staffordsville), on Paintsville Lake. 32 full-hookup sites (W/E/S, level gravel pads, RVs to ~48 ft, some pull-through) + 10 primitive + hike-in tent; restrooms, showers, laundry. Full-hookup loop within walking distance of the shoreline; several sites have direct lake views (e.g. site 18). Year-round. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks (ReserveAmerica). (RV Life mislabels it commercial; it is a state park.) RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Kentucky Horse Park Campground
note: State-owned (Kentucky Horse Park, Lexington; run by the Commonwealth, not the state-parks system). Large: 260+ paved sites, water + 20/30/50A electric, fire rings, + primitive area; bathhouses. Open to all campers (not horse-only). Year-round (B & D loops in off-season). Reserve via ReserveAmerica. (RV Life mislabels it commercial.) RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Pikeville RV Park
note: Local (City of Pikeville). Level concrete full-hookup pads (30/50A, continuous sewer), free cable + WiFi, daily-serviced trash; $25. In the city's rec corridor (dog park, courts, trails). RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Levi Jackson Wilderness Road Park
note: Local (City of London; a state park until 2019, now city-owned). Large wooded campground ~136-146 sites, water+electric at most (sewer at some), 3 bathhouse/laundry buildings, camp store; historic mill & museum on site. Some sites year-round. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Riverfront Park Campground
note: Local (City of Brandenburg), in Riverfront Park on the Ohio River. 16 sites, water + 20/30/50A electric, dump, free WiFi; two boat ramps, dock, river views. ~Apr 1-Nov 30, 10-day limit, $25. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Pulaski County Park
note: Local (Pulaski County), 804-acre park on Lake Cumberland (W Pulaski Co). RV sites (water+electric, upgrading to 50A) + primitive, near the lake; beach, boat ramps, courtesy dock, bathhouse, cabins, disc golf, trails. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Jericho Recreation Area
note: Local (Henry County), around 137-acre Lake Jericho (est. 1969). 62 RV sites (water+electric, ~half with sewer), dump, camp store; lakeside. ~Apr-Oct, first-come (nightly/weekly/monthly). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Moffit Lake Recreation Area
note: Local (Union County), on 64-acre Moffit Lake (~10 mi SE of Morganfield). 48 sites (30/50A + water), waterfront lake sites, 2 dump stations, 3 bathhouses + unlimited primitive. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: 2 Rivers Campground
note: Local (City of Carrollton), in Point Park at the Kentucky/Ohio river confluence. 33 full-hookup sites on level concrete pads (pull-through + back-in), bathhouse, playground, riverfront River Walk & pier; exceptionally clean. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Portal 31 RV Park
note: Local (Harlan County Fiscal Court / Tourist & Convention Commission), in Lynch next to the Portal 31 Exhibition Mine. 12 full-hookup sites + 8 primitive, big-rig friendly gravel, laundry, on-site coffee shop; year-round, $15. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Ohio County Park and Campground
note: Local (Ohio County), a 500-acre county park ~2 mi from Hartford. 84 sites (27 full-hookup, 5 pull-through; 50A water/electric/sewer); park lake, ball fields, ample rec. RV Life 2*. --Claude

name: Logan-Hubble County Park
note: Local (Garrard County), a 400-acre park/nature preserve between Stanford and Lancaster with a 12-acre lake. 13 gravel water+electric sites; disc golf, trails, shelters. ~Apr-Oct. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Marion Fair RV Park
note: Local (Marion County fairgrounds, Lebanon). 20 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water/sewer, gravel, max ~50 ft); open year-round for transient camping (not events-only). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Kentucky Splash Waterpark and Campground
note: Local (City of Williamsburg). Campground at the city's waterpark: 25 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water/electric/sewer, cable, WiFi) + 6 tent; year-round. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Mine Made Paradise Adventure Park & Campground
note: Local (Knott County), an ATV/horse adventure park on reclaimed coal land (Leburn/Vest). RV sites (water+electric) + cabins/tent, camp store, shower house; gateway to an extensive marked trail system. Open to all campers. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Sandhill RV Park & Camp
note: Local (McCreary County, Whitley City). 80 water+electric sites + primitive, dump station, showers, playground, shelter, community building. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Governor's Campground
note: Local (City of Manchester, Clay County), adjacent to Beech Creek Lake. ~75 sites (24 full-hookup concrete pads + 8 water/electric, rest primitive), dump, laundry, store, playground, courts, pavilions; year-round. RV Life 2.5*. --Claude

name: Myer Creek Park Campground
note: Local (McLean County, Calhoun). ~30 sites (12 with 50A + 12 with 30A water/electric, some water-only), day-use shelters; county park, RV sites being expanded. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Fish Pond Lake RV Park
note: Local (Letcher County), in 895-acre Little Laurel Park above 45-acre Fish Pond Lake (~1 mi W of Payne Gap, Jenkins). Graveled RV sites (water/sewer/30A), WiFi, restrooms (no showers); set above the lake (lake views, no direct water access), electric-motor/paddle boating. ~Mar-Oct. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Fox Valley Recreation Park
note: Local (Fleming County, Wallingford). 85 gravel sites, RVs to ~45 ft, 20/30A water/electric (some sewer), flush toilets, hot showers, dump, store; many seasonal/permanent sites plus a couple nightly-rental sites and a large primitive area. ~Apr-Oct, 14-day max. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Chain O'Lakes State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Spring Grove), at the Fox River chain of lakes. ~238 sites: Class A electric (Honeysuckle Hollow, Fox Den) + Class B-premium areas (Turner Lake South, Prairie View, Mud Lake East); showers, dump. Some sites by the water. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kankakee River State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Bourbonnais), on the Kankakee River. Potawatomi Campground: 110 Class A electric sites (showers, dump, flush toilets), RVs to ~35 ft; + Chippewa (Class BE/C). Wooded, set back from the river. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Rock Cut State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Loves Park/Rockford), at Pierce Lake. ~270 sites (199 electric across Prairie View/White Oak/Plum Grove/Stag Horn + 55 non-electric Hickory Hills); showers, dump, boat launch, playground. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct (walk-in Nov-Mar). RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lowden State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Oregon), on a bluff above the Rock River (Black Hawk statue). Sun Valley Area: 80 Class A electric (to 50A, shower, dump) + 38 Class B/S + hike-in. RVs/trailers over 40 ft not permitted. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Mississippi Palisades State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Savanna), bluffs above the Mississippi River. 241 sites, 110 electric (30/50A), max RV ~35 ft; showers May-Oct, dump. Campground set back atop the bluffs. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Lake Le-Aqua-Na State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Lena), on 45-acre Lake Le-Aqua-Na. 132 electric sites (some 50A) in Hickory Hill (paved, RV-friendly) + Pine Ridge; swim beach, boat ramp. Campground year-round; showers/dump May-Nov. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: White Pines Forest State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Mount Morris), along Pine Creek (fords/white pines). ~103 Class B/S drive-up sites (vehicular access, showers, dump) + only 2-3 non-reservable electric; $10. Small wooded park - best for compact rigs (a 23-ft fits; verify larger sites). FCFS + ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Apple River Canyon State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Apple River, NW IL). Canyon Ridge Campground: 49 Class C primitive sites (no hookups), pit toilets, water spigots, dump station, no showers; $8. ~mid-Apr to Oct, reservable + FCFS via ExploreMoreIL. Winding canyon access. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Morrison-Rockwood State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Morrison), on Lake Carlton. 92 electric sites (15/30A, grill, table; 59 reservable), shower May-Oct, dump. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnson-Sauk Trail State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Kewanee/Mineral), on Johnson Lake. Chief Keokuk Campground: 70 Class A electric pads + 25 tent; showers, dump, boat ramp, marina. Lakeside wooded setting. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Shabbona Lake State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Shabbona), on Shabbona Lake (muskie fishing). 150 Class A electric sites, showers, boat launch, store, playground. Many sites are small (not for big rigs; a 23-ft fits). Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Prophetstown State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Prophetstown), near the Rock River. 105 sites in two loops, all electric (some 50A); about half are full-hookup; dump, water hydrants, privies. Open/shaded. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Giant City State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Makanda), Shawnee hill country. 85 Class A electric sites (9 50A, rest 30A), water, showers, dump, privies; ~half reservable, rest FCFS via silent-host. $20. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL (closed Dec-Mar). RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Ferne Clyffe State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Goreville). Turkey Ridge Campground: North Loop (24-59) reservable electric (some 50A), South Loop (1-23) FCFS; + Deer Ridge (4 Class A, RVs to 70 ft). Showers, flush toilets, dump. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Fort Massac State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Metropolis), on the Ohio River (Illinois' first state park; reconstructed 1757 fort). 50 electric sites (30/50A, no water/sewer at sites), new bathhouse; boat dock/launch. Year-round (walk-in off-season). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Cave-in-Rock State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Cave-In-Rock), on a bluff above the Ohio River. 34 Class A electric sites (30/50A, units to ~60 ft), water, showers, flush toilets, dump. Campground set back - no river view from sites. First-come, first-served (no reservations). RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Dixon Springs State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Golconda), within Shawnee NF. 39 electric sites (30/50A; 2 pull-through), max RV ~40 ft, water, showers, dump, courts. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Lake Murphysboro State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Murphysboro), on Lake Murphysboro. 54 electric trailer sites (30/50A, electric-only), dump, showers; some back-in sites are right on the lake (mostly shorter rigs; max ~40 ft). + 20 tent. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Trail of Tears State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Jonesboro, 5,000 acres). 14 standard drive-in sites, no hookups, pads 25-130 ft (RVs of varying sizes fit), tables/fire rings, shelters with toilets/water; $8, year-round, reservable + self-register. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Fort Kaskaskia State Historic Site
note: Illinois DNR historic site (Ellis Grove), on a bluff overlooking the Mississippi/Kaskaskia. 32 electric-hookup sites (some walk-in) + primitive, water at select. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Randolph County State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Chester), on a 65-acre lake. Pine Ridge Campground: 146 sites - Class BE electric + some Class C full-hookup (water/sewer); RV limit ~40 ft, dump, restrooms, boat ramp; sites walk directly to the lake. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Pyramid State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Pinckneyville), reclaimed strip-mine with 500+ acres of lakes. 54 sites across 3 areas (Pyramid North, Boulder Lake, Jim Batson Trailer Area); no hookups (boondocking), max RV ~30 ft, pit toilets, tables/fire pits. First-come, first-served (no online reservations; not in the ExploreMoreIL system - verified 6/2026). RV Life 2*/$. --Claude

name: Sam Dale Lake State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Johnsonville), on 194-acre Sam Dale Lake. Two lakefront electric campgrounds - Hickory Hollow (33 sites, pads 25-58 ft) and Lake View (33 sites, pads 40-64 ft), both a few feet from the water; boat dock, playground, dump. Year-round. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Saline County State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Equality), overlooking Glen O Jones Lake. 45 sites, no electric/water hookups (water spigot near #31), trailer dump, pads 40-45 ft; boat ramp, swimming. First-come, first-served, ~Apr-Sept. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Fort Defiance State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Cairo), at the Ohio/Mississippi confluence. Small non-modern campground: 16 sites, 8 with electric, no flush toilets/showers, self-register; camp in the grassy area or on pavement down by the water. FLOOD-PRONE - the road closes during high water; call ahead (managed by Horseshoe Lake SFWA). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Eagle Creek State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Findlay), on a bluff overlooking Lake Shelbyville. 75 Class A reservable electric sites (water hydrants, showers, dump, fire blocks, tables); playground, boat ramp, visitor center. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. (State-park campground on the Corps lake.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wolf Creek State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Windsor), on Lake Shelbyville. 304 Class A electric sites (30A, gravel pads ~20-60 ft) + 78 Class C + cabin; showers, dump, potable water. Year-round; reserve May-Oct via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Eldon Hazlet State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Carlyle), western shore of Carlyle Lake (largest manmade lake in IL). Illini Campground: 328 Class A electric sites (30/50A) + 36 Class C + cabins; ~1.5 mi of shoreline, many loops within reach of the water (several with direct lake views and trails to sandy beach). Showers, dump, store, pool. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ramsey Lake State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Ramsey), alongside Ramsey Lake. ~91 sites, electric (30/50A; some water), pull-throughs; flush toilets, showers, dump. Sites 1-30 FCFS, rest reservable. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Stephen A. Forbes State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Kinmundy), on Forbes Lake. 115 electric sites (water + electric), max RV ~40 ft; showers, dump. Stairways + a floating walkway connect the campground to the marina/lake. Only one of three loops reservable, rest FCFS. (Separate equestrian campground too.) Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Hidden Springs State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Strasburg/Mode). Possum Creek Campground (Class C): no electric, flat drive-in sites (separate tent/RV sections), drinking water, privies, dump, playground, pavilion. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Sam Parr State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Newton), on 183-acre Sam Parr Lake. Lakeview Campground: paved back-in sites with 50A electric (water spigots, no hookups), RVs to ~45 ft; dump, restrooms; some sites overlook the lake. Year-round. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Red Hills State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Sumner, between Olney and Lawrenceville). 100+ Class A electric sites (water, dump, showers; some 50A pull-through), RVs to ~45 ft, big-rig friendly; wooded/private. 40-acre lake - some sites have lake views. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Beaver Dam State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Plainview, S of Carlinville). 59 electric-only sites (30/50A) under oak/hickory, 5 pull-throughs; shower building, dump, playground, pavilions; on-site vineyard. 59-acre lake (electric motors only) set below the campground. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Washington County State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Nashville), around a 248-acre lake. Shady Rest: 37 RV sites with 30/50A electric (+ tent areas); dump, water points, shower (Apr-Oct), toilet blocks; lake-loop trail. Cash only. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Horseshoe Lake State Park (Madison County)
note: Illinois DNR (Granite City), on a 2,400-acre Mississippi oxbow lake (Walker's Island). Semi-primitive: 26 sites, no electric/showers, water hydrants, dump, outhouses; $8. ~May-Sept. Distinct from Horseshoe Lake in Alexander County. RV Life 2.5*/$. --Claude

name: Argyle Lake State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Colchester). 159 sites: Twisted Oak (86 Class A, showers/electric, some full hookup), Big Oaks (24 Class B/E + 18 Class C), Twin Oaks (31 primitive); max RV ~35 ft, dump. On Argyle Lake (boat launch, dock, rentals). Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Delabar State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Oquawka), on the banks of the Mississippi River (89-acre park). 50+ RV sites with 30A electric (40 also tent), dump, water, restrooms, jet-pump fill; nearby marina/boat ramps. Year-round, permit from staff. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Big River State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Keithsburg, 3,000+ acres along the Mississippi). Shady Pines Campground: drive-in RV/trailer sites, no hookups, max RV ~40 ft, dump, water (Riverview area is tent-only by the river). First-come, first-served (not in ExploreMoreIL). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Nauvoo State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Nauvoo), near the Mississippi River; campground around 13-acre Lake Horton. 105 sites (35 Class A, 70 Class B), 30A electric (some water/sewer), showers, RVs to ~60 ft. ~Apr-Oct. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Siloam Springs State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Clayton/Liberty). 182 sites, 98 electric (30/50A, some full hookup), max RV ~45 ft. Pine Grove (49 Class A) + Hickory Hill (Class A + Class B); on a small lake with 6 fishing piers, camp store, boat rentals. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Starved Rock State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Oglesby/Utica), famous canyons above the Illinois River. 129 Class A Premium electric sites (30/50A), RVs ~15-45 ft; showers, dump. Campground set inland from the canyons/river. Reservation-only via ExploreMoreIL (fills fast). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Illini State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Marseilles), on the Illinois River. Great Falls + Whitetail campgrounds, electric (mostly 30A, shared boxes - bring an extension cord; no water at sites), dump; some sites have river views. Boat ramp. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Moraine View State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Le Roy), on 158-acre Dawson Lake. Gander Bay Campground: 137 Class A electric sites (large flat pads, RVs under ~35 ft, mostly back-in), showers, dump, water; some waterfront sites, boat ramp. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kickapoo State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Oakwood), a reclaimed mine with 22 deep-water ponds. 184 sites, ~half electric (sites 1-101, 30/50A; 50A sites on a ridge overlooking a lake); 2 shower buildings, dump, water. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Weldon Springs State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Clinton), on a 29-acre spring-fed lake. 75 Class A electric sites (30/50A, 5 pull-through) + 5 non-powered + 15 primitive; showers, dump, water. Year-round (reserve May-Sept). RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Fox Ridge State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Charleston), wooded ravines above the Embarras River. 43 electric sites (20/30/50A, no water/sewer), RVs to ~40 ft, back-in only; dump, showers (closed winter), water. Year-round, $20. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lincoln Trail State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Marshall), on 146-acre Lincoln Trail Lake. Two Class A campgrounds: Plainview (105 electric) + Lakeside (72 electric, many back-ins overlooking the lake), 30/50A; showers, double dump, water. RVs to ~44 ft. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Walnut Point State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Oakland), on 59-acre Walnut Point Lake. 34 electric sites (30/50A) in Grey/Fox Squirrel areas, many with lake views/close to the water; restrooms, showers, dump. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Weinberg-King State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Augusta). 29 Class B/E electric sites for general campers (+ 19 in a separate equestrian area), RVs to ~70 ft; tables, grills, water, toilets, dump, playground. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Jubilee College State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Brimfield, 3,200 acres). Class A electric + Class C sites; Coyote Cove loop has Class B electric open for winter camping. Showers, dump, graveled pads, water. Main season ~Apr 15-Nov 1. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Lincoln's New Salem State Historic Site
note: Illinois DNR historic site (Petersburg), by the reconstructed New Salem village near the Sangamon River. ~100 electric sites (30/50A) + ~100 non-electric, RVs to ~40 ft; hydrants (no water/sewer at sites), dump, two shower buildings. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Deer Run Campground (Sangchris Lake State Park)
note: Illinois DNR (Rochester), north side of Sangchris Lake. Deer Run Campground: 80 Class A electric sites (showers, dump) + 40 primitive + tent + equestrian; set in woods (not directly waterfront - Hickory Point is the lakefront campground, separately in DB). Closed mid-Jan to Mar. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Jim Edgar Panther Creek State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Chandlerville). Prairie Lake Campground (general): 84 electric sites incl. 19 full-hookup, some 50A; hot showers, restrooms. (Questing Hills is a separate horses-only campground.) Cabins, multiple fishing lakes. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Sand Ridge State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Forest City). Pine Camp: 24 drive-in sites in a pine plantation, no hookups; fire pads, tables, water spigots, vault toilets, dump across the road. Largest sites ~52-ft pull-through (most 25-45 ft). Reservable. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Woodford State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Low Point), on Illinois River backwater lakes (Goose Lake / Upper Peoria Lake). 12 seasonal drive-in sites near the boat ramps, no hookups, max RV ~30 ft; sheltered tables, pit toilets, fire pits. First-come, ~Apr-Sept. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Spring Lake State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Manito), on Spring Lake (Illinois River backwater). Pine + Oak campgrounds (Class C, no electric/showers), ~70 tent/trailer sites, trailer dump; + a walk-in tent area. First-come, first-served (not in ExploreMoreIL). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Anderson Lake State Fish and Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Astoria), on Anderson Lake (Illinois River backwater). North + South campgrounds, ~100 sites with waterfront access, tables/grills/water/toilets, dump; mostly no electric hookups, RVs to ~64 ft. First-come, first-served (not in ExploreMoreIL). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Forrest W. "Bo" Wood Campground
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (Sullivan, northern shore). 141 sites, all electric (pads to ~150 ft), + tent-only sites; flush toilets, showers, dump, playground, dog park, fish-cleaning station, high-water boat ramp. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Opossum Creek Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (western shore). 70 sites, most electric (RVs + tents, some tent-only non-electric); flush toilets, showers, playground, dump. Quiet. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Lone Point Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (western shore). Secluded; ~80 electric sites (pads to ~80 ft) + a separate group camp. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Coon Creek Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (western shore). Large: 181+ sites, all electric (pads to ~90 ft), ~half reservable; flush toilets, showers, swim beach, playground, bike pump track, basketball/volleyball courts, nature trail, dump, boat ramp. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Dam West Campground (Carlyle Lake)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Carlyle Lake (southern shore, by the dam; Illinois' largest man-made lake). 109 reservable electric sites (pads to ~138 ft); marina, boat storage; walk/bike across the dam to Dam East. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Dam East - McNair Campground (Carlyle Lake)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Carlyle Lake (east side of the dam). 47 individual sites (North/South loops 1-32 by the lake; East Spillway loop 33-47 below the dam) + a group shelter; swim beach, bathhouses, laundry. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Gun Creek Campground (Rend Lake)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (Whittington, near Wayne Fitzgerrell SRA). 98+ reservable electric sites (pads to ~85 ft); boating, fishing, swimming. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: South Marcum Campground (Rend Lake)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (SE shore). 158 reservable sites, electric (pads to ~94 ft) + tent sites; dump, hot showers, flush toilets, drinking water. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: South Sandusky Campground (Rend Lake)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (western shore, Sandusky cove). 124 sites: 119 electric (some full-hookup, pads to ~100 ft) + 8 tent; dump, hot showers, flush toilets; on the 20+ mi Rend Lake Bike Trail. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: North Sandusky Campground (Rend Lake)
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (western shore). 95 reservable electric sites (pads to ~66 ft); dump, hot showers, flush toilets, drinking water. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Fishermans Corner Campground
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (Hampton, Pool 14). 51 reservable sites: 46 electric (pads to ~80 ft) + 5 tent; amphitheater, canoe ramp. The Mississippi River Trail runs right past the campground. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Blanding Landing Recreation Area
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River backwaters (Hanover, NW IL). 38 sites, most electric (pads to ~50 ft); boat ramp, great fishing/birding. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Blanchard Island Recreation Area
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (New Boston). Small/primitive: 15 sites (table/fire ring, vault toilets; a couple of host sites have water/electric), boat ramp, view of Blanchard Chute. First-come 'Scan & Pay', ~May 15-Oct 15. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Andalusia Slough Recreation Area
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (Andalusia). Roadside park: campground + boat ramp + day use; vault toilets, water hydrants, group shelter, no electric. First-come, self-register $10, ~May-Oct. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Bear Creek Recreation Area (Mississippi River)
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (Ursa, near Quincy). FREE, first-come-first-served (14-day limit) -- NOT on recreation.gov; no reservation system. Primitive riverside sites: vault toilets, NO showers, NO fresh water (bring your own), fire rings, trash service, cement boat ramp; no hookups. Level, shaded gravel sites usable by RVs. On the riverside of the levee and flood-prone -- in spring confirm it isn't underwater (Rock Island District MS River Project Office 563-263-7913; local field office 217-228-0890). Verified GPS 40.1114,-91.4801 (Google Maps misroutes ~4 mi south). RV Life unrated, $. (Corrected: prior note's "hot showers / reservations required" was aggregator boilerplate -- firsthand 2015 RV report + snoflo's own data field confirm no showers and no reservations.) --Claude

name: Oak Point Campground (Lake Glendale)
note: USFS Shawnee NF, Lake Glendale Recreation Area (Glendale). 35 electric/water sites + 24 non-electric, RVs to ~40 ft; showers, dump station; swim beach on Lake Glendale. First-come, first-served, 14-day max. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Pine Ridge Campground (Pounds Hollow)
note: USFS Shawnee NF, on the ridgetop above Pounds Hollow Lake (Karbers Ridge). 13 electric (20/30/50A) + 22 non-electric sites, water spigot, $10 cash/check. Narrow undulating roads limit rigs to UNDER ~25 ft - a 23-ft fits but big rigs don't. FCFS, ~Mar 15-Dec 15. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Camp Cadiz Campground
note: USFS Shawnee NF (Elizabethtown), a former CCC work camp. Drive-in sites accommodating RVs to ~40 ft, no hookups; tables, fire grills, toilets, drinking water. Allows equestrians (general campground, not horse-only). FCFS, $10. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Redbud Campground (Bell Smith Springs)
note: USFS Shawnee NF, Bell Smith Springs Recreation Area (Ozark/Eddyville). 21 sites, no hookups, vault toilets, one potable-water source; accommodates pull-behinds/motorhomes to ~24 ft (a 23-ft fits; best for compact rigs). FCFS, $10, ~Mar 15-Dec 15. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Crab Orchard Campground (Crab Orchard NWR)
note: USFWS Crab Orchard National Wildlife Refuge (Marion), on Crab Orchard Lake; concessionaire-run (Bluegill Bay). 62 RV + 7 tent sites, full-hookup / electric-water / non-electric, showers, dump, flush toilets, small camper beach; many waterfront sites, RVs to ~38 ft. First-come, ~Apr 1-mid Nov. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnson Creek Recreation Area
note: USFS Shawnee NF, on a NW finger of Kinkaid Lake (Ava). 20 large drive-in sites, no hookups; boat ramp, toilets, drinking water. Allows equestrians (hitching posts/highline poles) but open to all; group sites take RVs to ~40 ft. FCFS, $10. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Pine Hills Campground (Shawnee NF)
note: USFS Shawnee NF (Wolf Lake), by the LaRue-Pine Hills bottoms. 13 level primitive sites, no hookups, vault toilets, no potable water (bring your own); max RV length ~80 ft but no pull-throughs. FCFS, $10, ~Mar 15-Dec 15. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Tower Rock Campground (Shawnee NF)
note: USFS Shawnee NF (Elizabethtown), on the bank of the Ohio River. Free primitive campground, pit toilet, no water/electric; improved sites take small trailers/RVs (~25 ft; a 23-ft fits). Flood-prone with river levels. ~May 1-Dec 15, 14-day max. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: World Shooting & Recreational Complex
note: Illinois DNR (Sparta), a 1,600-acre reclaimed-strip-mine shooting/event complex. ~1,001 campsites, all with 50A electric (premium + standard), showers, restrooms; open to general camping (busy during major shoots). Reserve ~Apr-Oct via ExploreMoreIL. (RV Life mislabels it commercial; it is IDNR.) RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Illinois State Fairgrounds Campground
note: Illinois Dept. of Agriculture (Springfield). ~300 sites with electric/water and limited sewer, showers, permanent restrooms; accommodates motorhomes/RVs/fifth-wheels/tents. Year-round (very busy during the State Fair and horse shows). (RV Life mislabels it commercial; it is state-owned fairgrounds.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Marion Campground & RV Park
note: Private (Marion), one block E of I-57 exit 53. 58 full-hookup sites, swimming pool, playground, game room, camp store, laundry; year-round. Easy on/off the interstate (some sites close together; mix of short-term and long-term). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Whittington Woods Campground
note: Private (Whittington), off I-57 exit 77, ~6 mi N of Benton, near Rend Lake. Pull-through full-hookup sites (50A, water/sewer), RVs to ~70 ft; wooded, quiet, level. Year-round. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: EZ Camp Now
note: Private (Benton), off I-57 near Rend Lake. 10 full-hookup sites (50A), WiFi, cable, well-lit/cameras, self-registration; geared to quick overnight stops (~$25). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Circle O Campground
note: Private (Fairfield). Large sites (power hookups + primitive), WiFi, fishing pond, zipline, event/group area, OHV trails; owners help park larger rigs. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Taylor Lake Campground
note: Private (Olney), just off IL-130. 10 full-hookup sites (15/20/30A, water/sewer), clean and well-kept; close to town amenities. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Arrowhead Campground (Robinson)
note: Private (Robinson), small park. 30/50A sites with water + septic hookups (no central toilets - self-contained rigs); clean, quiet, affordable overnight stop. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hickory Holler Campground
note: Private (West York), family RV park with two lakes/ponds. 80 sites (30/50A) + tent + cabins; swimming pool, playground, mini golf, fishing, camp store, trails. Year-round. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Pine View Campground
note: Private (Amboy), family-owned 36-acre wooded park. Full/partial-hookup + primitive sites (RVs to 45 ft), dump station, laundry, fishing/swimming ponds; reservations via RoverPass. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Skews seasonal but keeps a dedicated nightly/transient section. Ponds isolated from RV loops (waterfront none). RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Geneseo Campground
note: Private (Geneseo), 59 pull-thru + 12 back-in sites (RVs to 45 ft) plus cabins/yurt/store, 30/50A full & W/E hookups, 3 mi off I-80. On the Hennepin Canal trail (biking/fishing/paddling) but RV sites set back from the canal (waterfront none). Online reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Hauberg
note: Private (Port Byron), former-YMCA camp on a 40-acre wooded parcel along the Mississippi 2 mi N of town. 30A electric+water sites, pull-throughs, shower house, river fishing/boating; also rents lodges. River is an amenity, RV pads are wooded interior (waterfront none). Reservations by phone. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Leisure Lake Campground
note: Private (Rock Falls), small RV-only lakeside campground (not the membership Leisure Lake Resort in Joliet). Full hookups (sewer at every site), controlled access, fishing/kayak/canoe on Leisure Lake; explicit lakeside RV sites front the water. Daily ($30)/weekly stays. Open Apr 1-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ruffit Park
note: Private (Sterling, est. 1965), 120-site family campground: 95 electric/water 30/50A sites, 50 pull-throughs (RVs to 65 ft), 5 tent-only sites, swimming pool, laundry, dump station, store. Markets creek fishing but no confirmed creekside RV sites (waterfront none). Reservations via RoverPass. Open May 1-Oct 1. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kentuckiana Kampground
note: Private (Mackinaw), family campground with full-hookup sites (30/50A, sewer, concrete patio; rigs to ~50 ft), two stocked fishing ponds, laundry, live country-music concerts. Lakeside sites named are tent sites, RV sites not confirmed on the ponds (waterfront none). Reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Princess Wenona RV Oasis
note: Private (Wenona), 6 full-hookup (50A, water, sewer, WiFi) concrete pull-through pads at a truck-stop/mini-mart 1/4 mi off I-39 Exit 41; Burger King & Subway on site. Convenient overnight stop. First-come-first-served; no dedicated website (operating per consistent recent reviews). RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: D & W Lake Camping and RV Park
note: Private (Champaign), year-round 45-site full-hookup park (20/30/50A, pull-thru, big-rig to 70 ft) 5 mi from U of Illinois, with fishing lake, dog park, WiFi. Reviews confirm RV sites bordering/viewing the lake (waterfront lake). Water shut off at sites in early Nov. Online + phone reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Evening Star Camping Resort
note: Private (Topeka), ~300 sites on 35 acres (20/30/50A, pull-thru/back-in) with pool, mini-golf, fishing ponds, planned activities. Has a seasonal contingent but books public nightly via Campspot with no membership requirement. Ponds not confirmed RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp A While
note: Private (Lincoln), small ~11-site Route 66 transient park, open year-round, full + partial hookups (30/50A) $30-40/night, big-rig friendly (no on-site bathrooms). Reservations by call/email. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Prairie Pines Campground
note: Private (Rantoul), ~95-site 21-acre full-hookup park (water/sewer/electric, pull-thru, big-rig, laundry, clubhouse) on former Chanute AFB land, rates from $27. Mixed daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal use; takes daily transients. Reservations via RoverPass + phone. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Kamp Komfort RV Park & Campground
note: Private (Carlock), all full-hookup pull-thru sites (30/50A, up to 90 ft) near I-74 Exit 120, with pool, playground, covered group picnic area, 8 mi from Normal. Some highway noise. Reservations (call/RoverPass). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Countryside Campground
note: Private (Gibson City), year-round 52 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50A, leveled, big-rig to 60 ft) plus tent sites, with swimming pond/beach; $40/night. Pond is a rec amenity, no confirmed pond-side RV sites (waterfront none). Reservations by phone. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Deer Run Campground
note: Private (Golconda), small country campground in Shawnee NF; 30/50A full-hookup sites, tent sites, 4 primitive electric cabins, 3 bathhouses. Ohio River/swimming holes are nearby off-site attractions (waterfront none). Bookable (Hipcamp/phone). No standalone website (Facebook/Hipcamp), offset by strong reviews (Google 4.8). (Distinct from Deer Run at Sangchris Lake SP.) RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Bay Creek Ranch Campground
note: Private (Simpson), family-owned year-round Shawnee NF trail-riding ranch that also welcomes RVers/hikers (not equestrian-only); ~45 gravel sites with electric+water, dump station, bath house, horse stalls. No confirmed creekside RV sites (waterfront none). Online reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Cedar Lake Campground
note: Private (Vienna), large ~148-acre campground (200+ sites) on a 25-acre private lake bordering Shawnee NF; RV electric/water hookups, big-rig friendly, cabins, clubhouse, fishing; popular with horse riders (not equestrian-only). RV-site-to-lake relationship unconfirmed (waterfront none). Reservations via Campspot. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Four Seasons Campground
note: Private (Herrin), 38-site full-hookup town campground behind the city park; 19 pull-throughs handle any size RV, 20/30/50A, dump station, LP, laundry, storm shelter, 7 cabins. Small stocked pond is an amenity, not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Reservations (RoverPass). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hilltop Campground
note: Private (Goreville), year-round interstate-convenient campground at I-57 exit 40 (8 mi S of I-24); 35 sites (15 pull-thru/20 back-in) with W/E/sewer, big-rig friendly (~75 ft), WiFi, laundry. No water on site (waterfront none). Reservations required via ResNexus. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Robin Hood Woods
note: Private (Shelbyville), ~200 wooded full-hookup sites (W/S/E, 20/30/50A), big-rig pull-throughs, pool, store, laundry, cabins; accepts nightly + seasonal. Set back from Lake Shelbyville; on-site fishing pond is an amenity (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Spring Creek Campground
note: Private (Montrose), small family-owned gravel park ~1 mi N of Montrose just off I-70; 25 sites (18 electric-only + 6 elec/water, 30/50A, big-rig to 40 ft), dump station, showers, playground, fishing pond. Convenient I-70 overnight stop; pond is an amenity (waterfront none). No standalone website (Facebook); reservations via RoverPass. Open Apr-Nov. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tomahawk Campground
note: Private (Pocahontas), ~140 partial/full-hookup sites (15 pull-thru, 50A, up to 50 ft), showers, laundry, dump, 7-acre fishing lake; mix of seasonal + transient (confirmed overnight $25-30). No site-level RV-on-water confirmation (waterfront none). No standalone website (Facebook); reservations + walk-up. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kaskaskia Dragon RV Park
note: Private (Vandalia), small 12-site gravel park just off I-70; 30/50A electric + water, laundry, fits up to ~60 ft, novelty fire-breathing dragon attraction. In-town lot, no water frontage (waterfront none). Transient overnight stop; reservations by call. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Timber Trails Campground
note: Private (Mulberry Grove), family park 2 mi N of I-70 Exit 52; full hookups + 100-ft pull-thrus (up to 50 ft), pool, bathhouse, 3 fishing ponds, playgrounds; mix of seasonal + nightly (cash). Ponds dotted through park but no confirmed RV-on-water sites (waterfront none). Reservations. Open Apr 1-Nov 1. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lazy Days RV Park & Campground
note: Private (Litchfield, off old Route 66), 139 sites (standard 30A + premium/elite full-hookup), clubhouse, bathhouse, store, swimming lake + fishing ponds, kayaking. Official site markets 'elite' RV sites with waterfront views (waterfront lakeview). ~Half permanent residents but genuinely sells nightly stays. Reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Mt. CarMerriel RV Park
note: Private (Mount Carmel), small full-hookup park (30/50A), laundry/showers, near the local airport/golf; nightly/weekly/monthly (confirmed transient), open 24/7. Inland lot, no water frontage (waterfront none). Near Beall Woods SP. Reservations. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Percival Springs Airport & RV Park
note: Private (Watson), 24 full-hookup (30/50A) pull-thru grassy sites adjoining a private airstrip off US-45 (~8 min from I-57), laundry/showers, small on-site restaurant; fits long rigs. General drive-in RV park (not aviation-only); no water on site (waterfront none). Reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Country Bend Campground
note: Private (Litchfield, formerly Honey Bend Resort), 150+ sites + 13 cabins; pool, jumping pillow, putt-putt, four fishing ponds, pedal boats, game room. Family park near Route 66 / I-55. Ponds not confirmed RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Online booking via CampLife. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Driftwood Campground and RV Park
note: Private (Quincy, former KOA), ~51 sites, 50+ full-hookup (30/50A) mostly concrete pads, max ~50 ft, pool, laundry, showers, playground; on the Great River Road. Overlooks the Mississippi (river access across Bonansinga Dr; some sites have a distant river view, not on-water; waterfront riverview). Year-round; reservations via Campspot, 3-night min. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Timberview Lakes Campground
note: Private (Bushnell), ~60 full-hookup RV sites (30/50A), 11 big-rig pull-throughs (~60 ft), restrooms/showers/laundry, swimming lake + fishing ponds; public ('open to all', accepts Passport America). Water set back from RV sites (waterfront none). No clear official website (Good Sam/RoverPass); reservations by phone. Open Apr 15-Oct 15. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Timbered Meadows Campground
note: Private (Milford), ~95 W/E sites (no full hookups; dump station), bathhouses, fire pits, swimming, 3 fishing lakes, playground/courts; nightly overnight ($30-31) open to public plus weekly/monthly/seasonal. Lakes offer views but no confirmed RV-on-water sites (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservations by call. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sandy Oaks Campground
note: Private (Keithsburg), ~100 sites, E/W 20/30A, dump, laundry, cable, pool, playground, store, max RV ~50 ft; general campground that also offers equestrian sites (not horse-only) with a separate members-only ATV/motocross park. Direct access to Big River State Forest trails; no on-property water for RV sites (waterfront none). Reservations via Campspot. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Reinberg
note: Local (Forest Preserves of Cook County, in Deer Grove FP near Palatine). 5 individual drive-in RV/tent sites with 20/30/50A electric + water, gravel pads, fire rings; also cabins/tent/group sites. Year-round; reservations required. RV Life listed. --Claude

name: Camp Sullivan
note: Local (Forest Preserves of Cook County, Oak Forest — closest campground to downtown Chicago). 10 individual drive-in RV/tent sites, 30/50A electric, gravel pads ~32-38 ft (no water/dump). Year-round; reservations required. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Big Rock Campground
note: Local (Forest Preserve District of Kane County). 96 improved drive-in electric/water sites (handles 50-ft rigs), 2 dump stations, no showers; also tent + a few equestrian sites (general campground). Ponds/creek in preserve but not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Seasonal May 1-mid Oct; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Paul Wolff Campground
note: Local (Forest Preserve District of Kane County, in Burnidge FP near Elgin). 89 improved drive-in electric/water sites (handles 50-ft rigs), plus 5 equestrian + 10 primitive tent sites. Seasonal May 1-mid Oct; reservations required. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is county FP. --Claude

name: Blackwell Family Campground
note: Local (Forest Preserve District of DuPage County, in Blackwell FP, Warrenville). 60+ drive-in electric sites (tents/trailers/motorhomes), crushed-limestone pads, showers, dump + water-fill (no hookups). Silver Lake in preserve but not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Weekends-only May-Columbus Day; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Illiniwek Forest Preserve
note: Local (Rock Island County Forest Preserve District, Hampton) on the Mississippi. 60 water/electric RV pads + dump station + 2 shower houses; 25 primitive sites near the shore are tent-only. RV loops set back with river views (waterfront riverview). Seasonal Apr-Oct; first-come-first-served (permit at camp office). --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Hononegah Forest Preserve
note: Local (Winnebago County Forest Preserve District, Rockton) on the north bank of the Rock River. ~57 electric sites (20/30/50A), dump station, restrooms (no showers), playground; camp host registers on arrival. RV loop set back from the river (waterfront none). Year-round (water off in freezing temps); first-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Seward Bluffs Forest Preserve
note: Local (Winnebago County Forest Preserve District, Seward). 47 electric sites, dump station, ADA pit toilets (no showers), creek + limestone bluffs; separate equestrian loop (general campground). 30-ft max RV length (fits a 23-ft rig). Year-round; reservations required 7+ days out. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Sugar River Forest Preserve
note: Local (Winnebago County Forest Preserve District, Durand). ~70 electric sites in pine-shaded main loop + primitive river-side tent loop, showerhouse, 5.9-mi trails, fishing/paddling on the Sugar River. RV loop set back; on-river sites are tent-only (waterfront none). Year-round; main loop reservable, primitive FCFS. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Loud Thunder Forest Preserve
note: Local (Rock Island County Forest Preserve District, Illinois City). RV camping in White Oak (26 water/electric sites) + Deer Haven (35 full-hookup 50A pull-throughs); showers, marina/boat rental on 167-acre Lake George, 12+ mi trails; separate equestrian loop. RV loops not on the water (waterfront none). Reservable, 2-night min, Apr-Oct. RV Life ~9/10. RV Life mislabels state; it is county FP. --Claude

name: Forest Glen Preserve
note: Local (Vermilion County Conservation District, Georgetown). Family campground: ~34 electric sites (20/30/50A, no water/sewer at site) + 8 non-electric, water + 2 dump stations. Separate Vermilion River canoe/backpack camp is not RV. First-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Middle Fork Forest Preserve - Harry L. Swartz Campground
note: Local (Champaign County Forest Preserve District, Penfield). 65 sites (53 electric, 48 electric+water), ~$16/night, on-site host, flush toilets/showers, summer swim beach, 11 dark-sky sites; small ponds not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Reservations required. --Claude  RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Friends Creek Conservation Area
note: Local (Macon County Conservation District, near Decatur). ~35-40 sites (mostly 30/50A electric, 3 non-electric), showers/flush toilets/dump station; some back-in sites are short but 50A service handles larger rigs. Season ~May-Nov; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Comlara Park Campground
note: Local (McLean County Parks & Recreation, Hudson) on 925-acre Evergreen Lake. ~94 electric (mostly 30A, some 50A) + 23 non-electric + primitive walk-in; showers, dump station. Several RV sites sit directly on the lake with kayak-launch access (waterfront lake). Sites 1-33 year-round, rest Apr-Nov; reservations. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is county. --Claude

name: Sherwood Forest Campground
note: Local (City of Hillsboro), between Old Lake Hillsboro and Glenn Shoals Lake. Mostly seasonal (~113 permanent sites) but with 16 dedicated short-term/transient electric+water sites, dump station, online booking; $25-45/night. Transient sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). Apr 1-Oct 31; reservations via Firefly. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: South Park Municipal Campground
note: Local (City of Gibson City, in South Park at IL-9/Lott Blvd; some directories list this as 'Arrowsmith Park & Campground' — same place). 9 asphalt sites incl. 1 ADA pull-through, 30A electric + water, fire rings, restroom/showers, dump station. Apr-Oct, 7-night limit, ~$10-20; first-come-first-served, self-pay (shower keys at Police Dept). --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Twin Lakes Park
note: Local (City of Paris) on the Twin Lakes reservoirs (directory listings call it 'Paris City Park'). ~85 stoned sites, asphalt roads, 20/30/50A electric, dump station, showers/flush toilets, shelter. RV sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). Open all year (limited winter services); reservations incl. online via CampLife. --Claude

name: Fairview Park
note: Local (City of Casey). 40-acre municipal park, ~30 sites most with water + electric, dump station, fire pits, restroom/showers; $10-25/night. Twin fishing ponds not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). First-come-first-served, self-pay. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Jurgens Park
note: Local (Village of Arthur), south edge of town. 7 electric (30A) + water sites, bath house within walking distance, accommodates rigs to ~75 ft; $20/night. First-come-first-served (call Park Administrator). --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Robinson City Park
note: Local (City of Robinson Parks & Rec). 8 sites (first 3-4 with concrete pads), 20/50A electric + water spigot + dump station, pool/showers in park; $25/night. Open May-Sept; first-come-first-served, 3-night limit, pay at the pool. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Burrell Park and Campgrounds
note: Local (City of Carmi). 25 shaded concrete-pad sites, full hookups (30/50A water/sewer), modern bathhouse, WiFi; full amenities Apr 15-Oct 15 (winter camping allowed, showerhouse closed). Little Wabash River nearby but sites are park-interior (waterfront none). First-come-first-served; ~$16-25. RV Life ~9.9/10. --Claude

name: Charley Brown Memorial Park
note: Local (City of Flora), large 100-acre park ~3 mi W of Flora. ~85 grassy water/electric sites, modern restrooms/showers, pool, 9-hole golf, disc golf, small fishing lake (not RV-site-adjacent; waterfront none). First-come-first-served. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Main Brothers Campground
note: Local (Village of Karnak), near the Tunnel Hill State Trail / Cache River. 19 gravel sites all water/electric (50A on most, else 30A), 7 sewer sites + dump station, tent sites; ~$25/night RV. Reservations via Village Hall ($20 deposit). --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Arrowhead Lake Campground
note: Local (City of Johnston City), Arrowhead Recreation & Wildlife Area on a 30-acre lake off I-57 Exit 59. ~55-65 sites (some lakefront) all 30/50A electric + water, shower house, laundry, 2 cabins, nature trail; 40-ft max. A dozen RV sites sit directly on the lake (waterfront lake). Reservations recommended. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is municipal. --Claude

name: Bicentennial Campground - Lake Lou Yaeger
note: Local (City of Litchfield) at Lake Lou Yaeger. 18 wooded sites (1 ADA) with 30/50A + water spigot, dump station, playground, pavilion; nestled in woods ~1/2 mi from the shore (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Lake Jacksonville Campground
note: Local (City of Jacksonville Parks Dept) on ~500-acre Lake Jacksonville (directories misname it 'Lake Jackson'). Electric (20/50A) + water sites, dump station, fire rings, boat launch, marina, camp store. Seasonal-heavy mix but runs a real transient overnight program. RV sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). Apr 1-mid Oct; reservations by phone. --Claude  RV Life 3.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Pittsfield City Lake
note: Local (City of Pittsfield) on a ~200-acre city lake. ~33 sites, 30/50A electric + water, gravel pads, fire pits, shower house with flush toilets; fits up to 40-ft RVs. RV sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). ~$15 full-service. First-come-first-served, cash drop box. RV Life 4.0*. --Claude

name: Carthage Park District Campground
note: Local (Carthage Park District), next to the golf course / Carthage Lake. 15 sites all with water + electric, shelter house, near boat launch; lake is a short walk away, sites not on the water (waterfront none). ~$15 hookup. First-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Francis Park
note: Local (City of Kewanee). 60 RV sites (30x 30A, 30x 20A), central water fill + dump station, restrooms (no showers); $13/night. No water body at the park (waterfront none). First-come-first-served for electric sites; season closes ~end of August. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Allison Campground
note: Local (City of Galesburg) at Lake Storey Recreation Area. 142 sites (21 full-hookup, 101 electric/some water, 41 primitive/tent), concrete pads, dump station, adjacent water park/beach/golf. Official site map shows RV sites behind a wooded buffer, none on the shoreline (waterfront none). Season ~Apr-mid Oct; reservations by phone/in-person. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is municipal. --Claude

name: Keithsburg Riverside Campground
note: Local (City of Keithsburg) on the Mississippi. ~70 water/electric RV sites + 15 full-hookup, restrooms/showers, two dump stations, boat ramps; front-row sites sit on the river bank (waterfront river; riverfront row priced higher). Open May 1-Sep 30 (flood-dependent); first-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Sturgeon Bay Campground
note: Local (City of New Boston) on a Mississippi backwater (Sturgeon Bay). ~70 water/electric/sewer sites (30/50A), shower house, splash park, boat ramps; limited waterfront sites mostly held by seasonals so transient RV sites front-of-water not assured (waterfront riverview). Open Apr 1-Nov 1; reservations via City Hall. --Claude  RV Life 1*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Warsaw Riverfront Park
note: Local (City of Warsaw), Riverfront Park (a.k.a. Goose Landing / D&R Camp) on the Mississippi (Pool 20). 10 camping pads directly on the river bank, each with power + water, picnic table, fire ring; two boat ramps; dump station offsite. RV sites on the water (waterfront river). First-come-first-served, self-pay. --Claude  RV Life 3*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Village of Kampsville Campground
note: Local (Village of Kampsville) on the Illinois River. Full-hookup (E/W/S) and pull-through sites, shower house; ~$25/night RV. Riverside park but front-row RV placement not site-confirmed (waterfront riverview). Pay at village hall. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Great River Road Campground
note: Local (Pleasant Hill area, Pike County; adjoins the Pike County Fairgrounds but operates as a standalone public campground, not event-only). 59 full-hookup sites (E/W/S 30/50A), 40 pull-throughs, 60-ft max, dump station; inland by the fairgrounds (waterfront none). Open Apr-Oct; reservations by phone. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Roodhouse Community Park
note: Local (City of Roodhouse). ~20 full-hookup gravel sites (W/E/50A/sewer) + tent, shower house, laundry, dump station, boat ramp, stocked fishing lake, shelters/courts. Some sites near the lake but on-water RV placement unconfirmed (waterfront lakeview). Reservations by phone. --Claude  RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Fulton County Camping & Recreation Area
note: Local (Fulton County, on MWRD land leased & managed by the county, near Canton) on IDNR-stocked lakes. Electric (30A) and non-electric sites, shower house, dump, fresh-water pump; $30/night electric. Some seasonal sites on the lake; transient RV row on-water unconfirmed (waterfront lakeview). Open Apr 1-Oct 31; reservations. Coords approximate (office); camping loop is on the lakeshore. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Carl Spindler Campground & Marina
note: Local (Fon du Lac Park District, East Peoria) on the Illinois River (Upper Lake Peoria). 55 paved 50A sites (varying W/S) + ~27 primitive electric RV sites; electric-only/overflow sites sit on the river (waterfront river), marina, boat launch, pool, showers/laundry; some sites tight + road noise. Reservations via RoverPass. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is park-district. --Claude

name: Riverfront Park Campground
note: Local (Havana Park District) on the Illinois River next to the Havana Nature Center. 12 water/electric riverfront sites, firepits, playground, pavilions, dump station nearby; shore/dock fishing on site (waterfront river); $35/night. Reservations. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is park-district. --Claude

name: Pere Marquette State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Grafton), bluff country off Park Rd along the Great River Road near the Illinois River. Class A campground: 80 electric sites (20/30/50A), dump station, showers, drinking water; fits 25-68 ft rigs; year-round. Sites 2-30 reservable May-Oct via ExploreMoreIL, 31-80 FCFS; campground set inland behind trees (no water view from RV sites). --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Hamilton County State Fish & Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (near Enfield/McLeansboro), on L.P. Dolan Lake. ~61 Class B electric sites + 10 primitive (on the lakeshore) + 5 cabins; showers, water, dump station. Some electric RV sites + the primitive sites sit on the north shore (waterfront lake); roads/sites narrow (large rigs cautioned, 23-ft fine). Concession Apr 1-Oct 31, area year-round. Reservable via ExploreMoreIL or walk-in. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Henderson County Conservation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Gladstone), 87-acre area with a 27-acre fishing lake. Primitive drive-in tent/trailer sites on gravel pads by the lake (limited/older electric per reviews), pit toilets, tables. Camping area is lake-adjacent on a hill; on-water RV placement not confirmed (waterfront lakeview). Open year-round; first-come-first-served (permit on arrival). --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Green River State Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Harmon), rustic primitive vehicular camping in an open grassy/gravel area among grasslands/timber; no electric, no showers; dump station, water pump, vault toilets; gravel/blacktop access passable by a normal vehicle. RV/tent/van OK, very remote/dark-sky; self-pay ~$8/night, first-come-first-served. Camping area not on the river (waterfront none). Open year-round. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Allaire State Park
note: 45 tent/trailer sites; flush toilets and showers within walking distance; seasonal trailer sanitary station (dump). Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. Note: family campsites were closed for bathhouse construction as of 2025 — confirm reopening before booking. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Bass River State Forest
note: 176 tent/trailer sites by Lake Absegami (no hookups); flush toilets/showers, trailer sanitary station; open year-round. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Belleplain State Forest
note: 169 tent/trailer sites; water/electric hookups in the CCC loop only; flush toilets, showers, laundry; year-round trailer sanitary station; canoe concession on Lake Nummy. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Brendan T. Byrne State Forest
note: 82 tent/trailer sites with fire rings and picnic tables; flush toilets/showers within walking distance; pet sites 14–82. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cheesequake State Park
note: 50 tent/trailer sites; flush toilets/showers nearby; trailer sanitary station; 11-ft vehicle-height limit into the camp loop; open Apr 1–Oct 31. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Worthington State Forest
note: 78 sites along two miles of Delaware River frontage; sites 1–23 tent-only, sites 29–82 take travel trailers and motorhomes; showers, modern restrooms, drinking water, boat ramp; no pets/alcohol. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Parvin State Park
note: 56 tent/trailer sites along the south shore of Parvin Lake (no hookups); flush toilets/showers; trailer sanitary station. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Swartswood State Park
note: 65 sites; wooded sites are tent-only, the grass loop fits trailers/RVs (no hookups); flush toilets/showers/laundry; trailer sanitary + water stations; boat launch on Swartswood Lake; open Apr 1–Oct 31. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Voorhees State Park
note: 30 tent/trailer sites plus shelters and 2 group sites (no hookups); modern restrooms/showers; pets and alcohol prohibited; dump station shared at nearby Spruce Run; open Apr 1–Oct 31. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Stephens State Park
note: 39 tent and small-trailer sites along the Musconetcong River; flush toilets/showers nearby; open Apr 1–Oct 31. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Jenny Jump State Forest
note: 22 tent/trailer sites with fire rings and picnic tables; showers and toilets within walking distance; hilly forest with small sites; summit shelters and group sites also available; open Apr 1–Oct 31. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 3.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Stokes State Forest — Lake Ocquittunk
note: 26 trailer/tent sites plus 3 platform sites; the only Stokes loop with showers (flush toilets). Some RV sites sit on Big Flat Brook (creek) and some have a Lake Ocquittunk view; the lakefront sites themselves are tent-only. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Stokes State Forest — Shotwell
note: 27 trailer/tent sites plus lean-tos and platform sites; flush toilets, no showers; pet area; near Big Flat Brook. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life unrated (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wharton State Forest — Atsion Family Campground
note: Wharton's developed family campground on Atsion Lake; 50 tent/trailer sites (some lakeside, e.g. 4/8/10/12/20/22); potable water, flush toilets, showers, dump station; open year-round. Reservation system caps trailers at ~22 ft — tight for EKKO's 23 ft. Reservable online via NJ Outdoors. RV Life 3.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Turkey Swamp Park
note: Monmouth County Park System; 64 pull-through sites with water + electric (20/30 amp), fits rigs to ~34 ft; modern bathhouse, dump station; open Apr 1–Nov 15; most sites reservable online (2-night weekend min), some walk-in FCFS. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mahlon Dickerson Reservation
note: Morris County Park Commission; 18 paved back-in RV sites with electric (15/30/50 amp) and seasonal water (May 1–Oct 1), largest to ~40 ft; tent and Adirondack-shelter sites separate; highest-elevation campground in NJ; 14-night max; open Apr–Nov; reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Harmony Ridge Farm & Campground
note: Family-owned year-round campground in the Kittatinny Mountains; nightly and weekly RV reservations, 30/50-amp water+electric sites (no sewer; dump station on site); reviewers brought rigs up to ~40 ft; seasonals kept to separate areas. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude
